def_en: Exercising power in a cruel or arbitrary way; oppressive
quote_en: She never remembered seeing familiarly anything but the dark faces of her Ayah and the other native servants, and as they always obeyed her and gave her her own way in everything, because the Mem Sahib would be angry if she was disturbed by her crying, by the time she was six years old she was as tyrannical and selfish a little pig as ever lived. (Chapter 1)
quote_zh: 她从未熟悉地见过除了她保姆和其他当地仆人那黝黑面孔以外的任何事物。由于他们总是服从她，事事顺着她，因为如果她被她的哭闹打扰，夫人就会生气，到她六岁时，她已经变得像个专横自私的小猪一样了。
ex_en: True strength lifts others up; the tyrant builds walls, while the leader plants gardens where all can grow.
ex_zh: 真正的力量是帮助他人成长；暴君筑起高墙，而领袖种下花园，让所有人都能茁壮成长。

def_en: In a pleading or desperate manner
quote_en: All her clothes were thin and floating, and Mary said they were "full of lace." They looked fuller of lace than ever this morning, but her eyes were not laughing at all. They were large and scared and lifted imploringly to the fair boy officer's face. (Chapter 1)
quote_zh: 她所有的衣服都轻薄飘逸，玛丽说它们"满是蕾丝"。今天早上它们看起来比以往任何时候都更加蕾丝繁复，但她的眼睛却毫无笑意。它们睁得大大的，满是惊恐，哀求地望向那位金发年轻军官的脸。
ex_en: When words fail us, a gentle look can speak what the heart cannot yet say.
ex_zh: 当言语无法表达时，一个温柔的眼神能说出内心尚未能说出口的话。

def_en: A state of being confused or puzzled
quote_en: During the confusion and bewilderment of the second day Mary hid herself in the nursery and was forgotten by every one. (Chapter 1)
quote_zh: 在第二天的一片混乱和茫然中，玛丽躲进了育儿室，所有人都把她忘了。
ex_en: Life's greatest lessons often arrive wrapped in bewilderment, asking us to pause and grow.
ex_zh: 人生最深刻的道理，常常裹在困惑之中悄然到来，邀请我们停下来成长。

def_en: Not showing respect; boldly rude
def_zh: 放肆的；无礼的；厚脸皮的
quote_en: Basil was a little boy with impudent blue eyes and a turned-up nose and Mary hated him. (Chapter 2)
quote_zh: 巴兹尔是个蓝眼睛里透着无礼、鼻子翘翘的小男孩，玛丽讨厌他。
ex_en: True confidence needs no rudeness; kindness speaks louder than an impudent tongue.
ex_zh: 真正的自信不需要无礼；善良比放肆的话语更有力量。

def_en: Opposite in nature; inclined to oppose or disagree stubbornly
def_zh: 相反的；倔强的；故意作对的
quote_en: "Mistress Mary, quite contrary, How does your garden grow? With silver bells, and cockle shells, And marigolds all in a row." (Chapter 2)
quote_zh: "玛丽小姐，真别扭，你的花园种得怎么样？种着银铃铛，种着海贝壳，还有一排排金盏花。"
ex_en: A contrary spirit may close the door to friendship, but an open heart invites it in.
ex_zh: 倔强的心会把友谊之门关上，而敞开的心扉则会邀请友谊进来。

def_en: A strong feeling of contempt or disdain
quote_en: "She doesn't know where home is!" said Basil, with seven-year-old scorn. "It's England, of course." (Chapter 2)
quote_zh: "她不知道家在哪里！"巴兹尔带着七岁孩童的轻蔑说道。"当然是英格兰。"
ex_en: A wise heart learns from others; a closed mind only knows how to scorn.
ex_zh: 智慧的心向他人学习；封闭的头脑只知道轻蔑。

def_en: Barren, dreary, or feeling abandoned and lonely
quote_en: "He lives in a great, big, desolate old house in the country and no one goes near him." (Chapter 2)
quote_zh: "他住在乡下那座巨大而荒凉的老宅子里，没有人靠近他。"
ex_en: Even the most desolate place can bloom again with patience, care, and hope.
ex_zh: 即使是最荒凉的地方，也能在耐心、关怀和希望中重新绽放。

def_en: Cold and unemotional; showing no sympathy
def_zh: 冷漠的；无情的；铁石心肠的
quote_en: She looked so stony and stubbornly uninterested that they did not know what to think about her. (Chapter 2)
quote_zh: 她看起来如此冷漠、固执地毫无兴趣，以至于他们不知道该怎么看她。
ex_en: A stony heart may protect itself from pain, but it also shuts out the warmth of love.
ex_zh: 冷漠的心或许能保护自己免受伤害，却也把爱的温暖拒之门外。

def_en: Having a pale, yellowish complexion, often from illness
def_zh: 蜡黄的；灰黄的；病黄色的
quote_en: "Perhaps she will improve as she grows older," the officer's wife said good-naturedly. "If she were not so sallow and had a nicer expression, her features are rather good." (Chapter 2)
quote_zh: "等她长大些也许会变好，"军官的妻子和善地说。"如果不是这么蜡黄，表情再好看些，她的五官还是挺不错的。"
ex_en: True beauty blooms from within; a cheerful heart brings color to any face.
ex_zh: 真正的美丽由内而外绽放；快乐的心能为任何面孔带来光彩。

def_en: To grow or spread in an untidy, irregular way
quote_en: Her black dress made her look yellower than ever, and her limp light hair straggled from under her black crêpe hat. (Chapter 2)
quote_zh: 她的黑裙子让她看起来比以往更黄，她那耷拉的浅色头发从黑色绉纱帽下散乱地垂落。
ex_en: A garden left untended may straggle, but with gentle hands it finds its shape again.
ex_zh: 无人照料的花园也许会杂乱无章，但在温柔的双手下它会重获生机。

def_en: Easily annoyed or irritated; peevish
def_zh: 易怒的；闹脾气的；任性的
quote_en: "A more marred-looking young one I never saw in my life," Mrs. Medlock thought. (Marred is a Yorkshire word and means spoiled and pettish.) (Chapter 2)
quote_zh: "我这辈子没见过比这更不招人喜欢的孩子了，"梅德洛克太太想。（"Marred"是个约克郡词，意思是被宠坏、爱发脾气。）
ex_en: A pettish child may find friends hard to keep, but kindness learned young becomes a lifelong treasure.
ex_zh: 爱发脾气的孩子或许很难交到朋友，但从小学会的善良会成为一生的财富。

def_en: Not reacting or answering; indifferent
def_zh: 无反应的；冷淡的；不感兴趣的
quote_en: "Humph," muttered Mrs. Medlock, staring at her queer, unresponsive little face. (Chapter 2)
quote_zh: "哼，"梅德洛克太太咕哝着，盯着她那古怪、毫无反应的小脸。
ex_en: The world feels cold to those who shut themselves away, but every door opens to a knock of courage.
ex_zh: 对那些将自己封闭起来的人，世界是冰冷的，但每扇门都会为勇敢的叩击而打开。

def_en: To make someone feel uneasy or embarrassed
quote_en: Mary said nothing at all, and Mrs. Medlock looked rather discomfited by her apparent indifference, but, after taking a breath, she went on. (Chapter 2)
quote_zh: 玛丽什么也没说，梅德洛克太太被她明显的冷漠弄得有些窘迫，但吸了口气后，她还是继续说了下去。
ex_en: When we expect a warm response and meet cold silence, we need not be discomfited—for every heart warms in its own time.
ex_zh: 当我们期待热情的回应却遭遇冰冷的沉默时，不必感到窘迫——因为每颗心都会在它自己的时间里温暖起来。

def_en: A large area of open, uncultivated land, often covered with heather
quote_en: The house is six hundred years old and it's on the edge of the moor, and there's near a hundred rooms in it, though most of them's shut up and locked. (Chapter 2)
quote_zh: 这房子有六百年历史，坐落在荒野边缘，里面有将近一百个房间，尽管大部分都锁着。
ex_en: Like the wide moor stretching to the sky, our hearts have room to roam and grow beyond what we can see.
ex_zh: 就像伸展向天际的广阔荒野，我们的心也有足够的空间去漫游、成长，超越我们所能看见的边界。

def_en: A thing of little value or importance; a small amount
quote_en: She had never thought of the hunchback's being married and she was a trifle surprised. (Chapter 2)
quote_zh: 她从未想过那个驼背会结婚，所以感到有些意外。
ex_en: No kindness is a trifle; the smallest seed may grow into a mighty garden.
ex_zh: 没有哪一种善意是微不足道的；最小的种子也能长成宏伟的花园。

def_en: Bare, desolate, and often windswept; grim or depressing
quote_en: On and on they drove through the darkness, and though the rain stopped, the wind rushed by and whistled and made strange sounds... Mary felt as if the drive would never come to an end and that the wide, bleak moor was a wide expanse of black ocean through which she was passing on a strip of dry land. (Chapter 3)
quote_zh: 他们在黑暗中一路前行，虽然雨停了，风却呼啸着，发出奇怪的声音……玛丽觉得这段路程仿佛永远没有尽头，那片广阔、荒凉的荒野就像一片黑色的汪洋，而她正从一条干涸的陆地上穿行而过。
ex_en: Even the bleakest path leads somewhere new, and winter always yields to spring.
ex_zh: 即使是最荒凉的道路也会通向新的地方，冬天总会让位给春天。

def_en: Obedient or attentive to an excessive or servile degree
def_zh: 谄媚的；奉承的；卑躬屈膝的
quote_en: The native servants she had been used to in India were not in the least like this. They were obsequious and servile and did not presume to talk to their masters as if they were their equals. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: 她在印度习惯了的当地仆人完全不是这样。他们谄媚、卑躬屈膝，不敢以平等的身份和主人说话。
ex_en: True friendship grows between equals; no one can be a friend to another while kneeling.
ex_zh: 真正的友谊在平等的人之间生长；没有人能跪着和别人做朋友。

def_en: Arrogantly superior and disdainful
quote_en: "You are a strange servant," she said from her pillows, rather haughtily. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: "你是个奇怪的仆人，"她从枕头上颇为高傲地说道。
ex_en: A haughty heart stands alone on a high hill, never knowing the warmth of the valley below.
ex_zh: 傲慢的心独自矗立在高山上，永远不知道山谷里的温暖。

def_en: Arrogantly commanding; domineering
def_zh: 专横的；傲慢的；发号施令的
quote_en: "Are you going to be my servant?" Mary asked, still in her imperious little Indian way. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: "你是要做我的仆人吗？"玛丽问道，语气仍然是她在印度时那专横的小模样。
ex_en: The imperious command may be obeyed, but the gentle request wins a willing heart.
ex_zh: 专横的命令或许会被服从，但温柔的请求却能赢得一颗心甘情愿的心。

def_en: In a firm, determined, and resolute manner
quote_en: "I'm Mrs. Medlock's servant," she said stoutly. "An' she's Mr. Craven's—but I'm to do the housemaid's work up here an' wait on you a bit." (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: "我是梅德洛克太太的仆人，"她坚定地说。"她是克莱文先生的——但我负责楼上的女仆工作，顺便照看你一些。"
ex_en: Those who stand stoutly for what is right may walk alone for a time, but they walk with courage.
ex_zh: 那些坚定捍卫正确之事的人或许会独自走一段路，但他们与勇气同行。

def_en: In a manner showing anger or annoyance at unfair treatment
quote_en: "No," answered Mary, quite indignantly. "I never did in my life. My Ayah dressed me, of course." (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: "不，"玛丽颇为愤慨地回答。"我这辈子都没自己穿过。当然是我的保姆给我穿的。"
ex_en: To learn for oneself is to grow; to wait for others is to stay small.
ex_zh: 自己学习就是成长；等待别人就会停滞不前。

def_en: With a manner showing contempt or lack of respect
quote_en: "It is different in India," said Mistress Mary disdainfully. She could scarcely stand this. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: "在印度可不一样，"玛丽小姐轻蔑地说。她几乎受不了这个。
ex_en: The truly wise do not look down on what they do not know; they bend to learn.
ex_zh: 真正有智慧的人不会轻视他们不了解的事物；他们俯身去学习。

def_en: Not held back; uncontrolled or free
def_zh: 无拘束的；放纵的；奔放的
quote_en: She sobbed so unrestrainedly that good-natured Yorkshire Martha was a little frightened and quite sorry for her. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: 她哭得那样毫无顾忌，好心的约克郡姑娘玛莎有点害怕，也十分同情她。
ex_en: Tears unrestrained can wash away sorrow and make room for joy to enter.
ex_zh: 毫无保留的泪水能冲走悲伤，为喜悦腾出空间。

def_en: Prepared to obey others unquestioningly; servile
quote_en: If Martha had been a well-trained fine young lady's maid she would have been more subservient and respectful and would have known that it was her business to brush hair, and button boots, and pick things up and lay them away. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: 如果玛莎是个训练有素的年轻小姐贴身女仆，她会更顺从、更恭敬，也会知道她的职责是梳头、扣靴子、捡东西并收好。
ex_en: True care is not subservient; it is the willing hand that helps another stand tall.
ex_zh: 真正的关怀不是卑躬屈膝，而是愿意伸出援手，帮助他人站得更直。

def_en: Relating to the countryside; simple and unsophisticated
quote_en: She was, however, only an untrained Yorkshire rustic who had been brought up in a moorland cottage with a swarm of little brothers and sisters... (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: 然而，她只是个未经训练的约克郡乡下姑娘，在荒野的小屋里和一群弟弟妹妹一起长大……
ex_en: A rustic heart, simple and true, often holds more wisdom than polished words can say.
ex_zh: 一颗朴实真挚的乡村之心，往往蕴含着比华丽辞藻更深的智慧。

def_en: Simple but cozy and comfortable; plain or unpretentious
quote_en: At first she was not at all interested, but gradually, as the girl rattled on in her good-tempered, homely way, Mary began to notice what she was saying. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: 起初她一点也不感兴趣，但随着女孩用她那好脾气、朴实的方式喋喋不休地说着，玛丽开始留意她在说什么。
ex_en: There is beauty in the homely things of life—a warm fire, a kind word, a shared loaf of bread.
ex_zh: 生活中朴素的事物自有其美——温暖的火炉、善意的话语、共同分享的面包。

def_en: A view or attitude toward something; a tender feeling
quote_en: Mary had never possessed an animal pet of her own and had always thought she should like one. So she began to feel a slight interest in Dickon, and as she had never before been interested in any one but herself, it was the dawning of a healthy sentiment. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: 玛丽从未拥有过自己的宠物，一直觉得自己应该会喜欢一个。于是她开始对迪肯产生了一点兴趣，由于她以前除了自己从未对任何人产生过兴趣，这是一种健康情感的萌芽。
ex_en: The first step outside ourselves is the longest; after that, kindness becomes a habit.
ex_zh: 走出自我的第一步最为漫长；之后，善良便成了一种习惯。

def_en: Of considerable size, amount, or quality; hearty
quote_en: A table in the center was set with a good substantial breakfast. But she had always had a very small appetite, and she looked with something more than indifference at the first plate Martha set before her. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: 中间的桌子上摆着一顿相当丰盛的早餐。但她一向胃口很小，对于玛莎放在她面前的第一个盘子，她带着不止是冷漠的表情看着。
ex_en: A substantial meal feeds the body, but a substantial kindness feeds the soul for a lifetime.
ex_zh: 丰盛的饭菜滋养身体，而丰厚的善意滋养灵魂一生。

def_en: Lack of interest, concern, or sympathy
quote_en: A table in the center was set with a good substantial breakfast. But she had always had a very small appetite, and she looked with something more than indifference at the first plate Martha set before her. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: 中间的桌子上摆着一顿相当丰盛的早餐。但她一向胃口很小，对于玛莎放在她面前的第一个盘子，她带着不止是冷漠的表情看着。
ex_en: Indifference is a wall that shuts out the world; caring is the door that lets it in.
ex_zh: 冷漠是一堵将世界拒之门外的墙；关怀是让世界走进来的门。

def_en: Molasses or golden syrup; a thick, sweet syrup
quote_en: "Tha' doesn't want thy porridge!" Martha exclaimed incredulously. "No." "Tha' doesn't know how good it is. Put a bit o' treacle on it or a bit o' sugar." (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: "你不想喝粥！"玛莎难以置信地叫道。"不想。""你不知道它有多好。放点糖浆或者放点糖。"
ex_en: Even the plainest food is sweetened by a grateful heart.
ex_zh: 即使是最朴素的食物，也会因一颗感恩的心而变得甜美。

def_en: Food or provisions (often used in plural)
quote_en: "Eh!" said Martha. "I can't abide to see good victuals go to waste. If our children was at this table they'd clean it bare in five minutes." (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: "哎！"玛莎说。"我可看不下去好好的食物被浪费。要是我们家孩子在这张桌子前，五分钟就能吃得干干净净。"
ex_en: To share one's victuals with the hungry is to share one's heart.
ex_zh: 与饥饿的人分享食物，就是分享自己的心。

def_en: Bad-tempered and unfriendly; rude
def_zh: 脾气坏的；粗暴的；不友善的
quote_en: Presently an old man with a spade over his shoulder walked through the door leading from the second garden. He looked startled when he saw Mary, and then touched his cap. He had a surly old face, and did not seem at all pleased to see her... (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: 不一会儿，一个扛着铲子的老人从第二个花园的门里走出来。他看到玛丽时显得很惊讶，然后碰了碰帽檐。他有一张粗鲁苍老的脸，见到她似乎一点也不高兴……
ex_en: Behind a surly face may live a heart as soft as summer earth, waiting for spring.
ex_zh: 在粗暴的面孔背后，或许藏着一颗如夏日泥土般柔软的心，只待春天。

def_en: Easily irritated; curt and surly
def_zh: 易怒的；粗暴的；脾气坏的
quote_en: "There was nothin' to prevent thee," he answered crustily. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: "又没什么拦着你，"他粗暴地回答。
ex_en: A crusty shell may protect a tender heart, but it keeps kind hands at a distance.
ex_zh: 粗硬的外壳或许能保护柔软的心，却也把善意的手挡在远处。

def_en: To descend and settle; to land or perch
quote_en: Almost the next moment a wonderful thing happened. She heard a soft little rushing flight through the air—and it was the bird with the red breast flying to them, and he actually alighted on the big clod of earth quite near to the gardener's foot. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: 紧接着，奇妙的事情发生了。她听到空中传来一阵轻柔的飞掠声——是那只红胸脯的鸟儿正向他们飞来，它真的落在离园丁脚很近的一大块土上。
ex_en: Trust, like a bird, alights only where it finds a heart it knows is safe.
ex_zh: 信任，就像鸟儿一样，只会在它认为安全的地方停留。

def_en: Impudent or irreverent in a playful way
def_zh: 厚脸皮的；放肆的；冒失的
quote_en: "Where has tha' been, tha' cheeky little beggar?" he said. "I've not seen thee before to-day." (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: "你跑哪儿去了，你这厚脸皮的小家伙？"他说。"我今天之前都没见过你。"
ex_en: A cheeky spirit may raise a laugh, but a kind one raises a friend.
ex_zh: 冒失的性情或许能引来笑声，但善良的性情却能交到朋友。

def_en: Very fine in texture or structure; easily broken; graceful
quote_en: He had a tiny plump body and a delicate beak, and slender delicate legs. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: 它有着胖乎乎的小身子、精致的喙，还有纤细娇弱的腿。
ex_en: Even the most delicate thing can endure if it is loved and cared for.
ex_zh: 即使是最娇弱的事物，只要被爱护和照料，也能坚强地存活。

def_en: A young bird that has just learned to fly; a beginner
quote_en: "Aye, that he will. I've knowed him ever since he was a fledgling. He come out o' th' nest in th' other garden..." (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: "是的，他会来。我从它还是只雏鸟时就认识它了。它从另一个花园的巢里孵出来……"
ex_en: Every fledgling must learn to fly; fear is the nest, courage is the sky.
ex_zh: 每一只雏鸟都要学会飞翔；恐惧是巢穴，勇气是天空。

def_en: A family of young animals, especially birds
quote_en: When he went over th' wall again th' rest o' th' brood was gone an' he was lonely an' he come back to me. (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: 等它再飞过墙去，那一窝其他的鸟都走了，它很孤单，就又回到我这儿来了。
ex_en: A scattered brood finds its way, and loneliness, too, can be a path to friendship.
ex_zh: 四散的雏鸟各寻其路，而孤独，也能成为通往友谊的道路。

def_en: To guarantee or promise; to justify or deserve
quote_en: "I warrant th' foxes shows him where their cubs lies an' th' skylarks doesn't hide their nests from him." (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: "我敢说，狐狸会告诉他幼崽藏在哪儿，云雀也不会对他隐瞒自己的巢。"
ex_en: A heart that loves all creatures will find the world full of friends—I warrant it.
ex_zh: 一颗热爱所有生灵的心会发现世界充满朋友——我敢保证。

def_en: Fond of interfering in other people's affairs
quote_en: "None as any one can find, an' none as is any one's business. Don't you be a meddlesome wench an' poke your nose where it's no cause to go." (Chapter 4)
quote_zh: "没有谁能找到的门，也不是谁该管的事。你别做个爱管闲事的丫头，把鼻子伸到不该伸的地方去。"
ex_en: Curiosity that respects boundaries builds bridges; meddlesome ways build only walls.
ex_zh: 尊重边界的探索能架起桥梁；爱管闲事只会筑起高墙。

def_en: To groom feathers with the beak; to show self-satisfaction
quote_en: Then she ran down the path through the other door and then into the orchard, and when she stood and looked up there was the tree on the other side of the wall, and there was the robin just finishing his song and beginning to preen his feathers with his beak. (Chapter 5)
quote_zh: 然后她顺着小径穿过另一扇门，进入果园，当她站着抬头看时，墙另一边的那棵树就在那里，知更鸟刚刚唱完歌，开始用嘴整理羽毛。
ex_en: To preen is to care for oneself, but a heart that cares only for itself will never know the joy of caring for another.
ex_zh: 梳理羽毛是爱惜自己，但只爱惜自己的心永远不会懂得关爱他人的快乐。

def_en: Slow and relaxed; lacking energy or vitality
quote_en: In India she had always felt hot and too languid to care much about anything. (Chapter 5)
quote_zh: 在印度，她总是觉得很热，倦怠得对什么都提不起兴趣。
ex_en: When the spirit is languid, it takes but a ray of sunshine or a kind word to waken it again.
ex_zh: 当精神倦怠时，一缕阳光或一句善意的话语就足以将它重新唤醒。

def_en: The quality of being new, original, or unusual
quote_en: Martha liked to talk, and the strange child who had lived in India, and been waited upon by "blacks," was novelty enough to attract her. (Chapter 5)
quote_zh: 玛莎喜欢说话，而这个在印度长大、被"黑人"伺候着的奇怪孩子，足够新奇，吸引了她。
ex_en: Novelty may catch the eye, but kindness holds the heart.
ex_zh: 新奇或许能吸引眼球，但善良才能留住人心。

def_en: To continue firmly despite difficulty or opposition
quote_en: "Why did he hate it?" Mary persisted. (Chapter 5)
quote_zh: "他为什么恨那个花园？"玛丽追问道。
ex_en: Questions that persist are seeds of understanding; answers will come to those who ask with patience.
ex_zh: 坚持追问的问题是理解的种子；答案会降临在有耐心的人身上。

def_en: To strike repeatedly; to struggle against
quote_en: It must mean that hollow shuddering sort of roar which rushed round and round the house as if the giant no one could see were buffeting it and beating at the walls and windows to try to break in. (Chapter 5)
quote_zh: 那一定是指那种空洞的、令人战栗的呼啸声，绕着房子来回冲击，仿佛那个看不见的巨人在反复撞击它，捶打着墙壁和窗户，试图闯进来。
ex_en: Life will buffet us with storms, but we build our shelter in courage and friendship.
ex_zh: 生活会用风暴冲击我们，但我们在勇气和友谊中建造避风港。

def_en: To recognize as different; to perceive clearly
quote_en: She did not know what it was, because at first she could scarcely distinguish it from the wind itself. (Chapter 5)
quote_zh: 她不知道那是什么，因为起初她几乎无法将它和风声区分开来。
ex_en: To distinguish truth from fear is to find the courage to listen—and to act.
ex_zh: 区分真相与恐惧，就是找到倾听的勇气——然后去行动。

def_en: A strong, fast-flowing stream of water or other liquid
quote_en: The next day the rain poured down in torrents again, and when Mary looked out of her window the moor was almost hidden by gray mist and cloud. (Chapter 6)
quote_zh: 第二天又下起了倾盆大雨，玛丽从窗户望出去，荒野几乎被灰色的雾和云遮蔽了。
ex_en: After the torrent, the sun returns, and what was drowned springs forth anew.
ex_zh: 急流过后，太阳会回来，被淹没的一切会重新焕发生机。

def_en: To feel bitterness or indignation at something unfair
quote_en: The time had come when Mary had forgotten to resent Martha's familiar talk. (Chapter 6)
quote_zh: 玛丽已经忘了要反感玛莎那随意的谈话了。
ex_en: To let go of resentment is to open a window in a stuffy room; fresh air rushes in.
ex_zh: 放下怨恨，就像在闷热的房间里打开一扇窗；新鲜空气会涌入。

def_en: A rich fabric woven with a raised pattern
quote_en: There was a stiff, plain little girl rather like herself. She wore a green brocade dress and held a green parrot on her finger. (Chapter 6)
quote_zh: 有一个僵硬、朴素的小女孩，和她颇为相像。她穿着绿色锦缎裙子，手指上站着一只绿鹦鹉。
ex_en: Fine brocade may clothe a person, but kindness makes them beautiful.
ex_zh: 华丽的锦缎可以装扮一个人，但善良才让他们真正美丽。

def_en: A covered litter carried on poles, used in India and other Asian countries
quote_en: In one room, which looked like a lady's sitting-room, the hangings were all embroidered velvet, and in a cabinet were about a hundred little elephants made of ivory. They were of different sizes, and some had their mahouts or palanquins on their backs. (Chapter 6)
quote_zh: 在一个像是女士起居室的房间里，帷幔都是绣花天鹅绒的，柜子里有大约一百只象牙小象。它们大小不一，有些背上还有骑手或轿子。
ex_en: The grandest possessions bring no comfort if the heart is empty; a simple joy fills the soul.
ex_zh: 最华丽的财富若心中空虚也带不来安慰；简单的快乐却能充盈灵魂。

def_en: With a feeling of vague or regretful longing
quote_en: "Could I ever get there?" asked Mary wistfully, looking through her window at the far-off blue. (Chapter 7)
quote_zh: "我能到那儿去吗？"玛丽渴望地问，透过窗户望着远处的那片蓝色。
ex_en: A wistful heart dreams of what may be; a brave one takes the first step to make it so.
ex_zh: 惆怅的心梦想着可能发生的事；勇敢的心迈出第一步去实现它。

def_en: The action or power of remembering
quote_en: Martha grinned a little as if at some homely recollection. "Mother said that to me once," she said. (Chapter 7)
quote_zh: 玛莎笑了笑，仿佛想起了某件家常往事。"妈妈曾经对我这么说过，"她说。
ex_en: The sweetest recollections are not of grand things, but of small kindnesses shared with love.
ex_zh: 最甜美的回忆并非关于宏大的事物，而是那些充满爱意分享的小小善举。

def_en: Impudently lively and bold; cheeky
def_zh: 无礼的；冒失的；活泼大胆的
quote_en: Very soon she heard the soft rustling flight of wings again and she knew at once that the robin had come again. He was very pert and lively, and hopped about so close to her feet... (Chapter 7)
quote_zh: 很快她又听到了翅膀轻柔的沙沙声，她立刻知道知更鸟又来了。它非常活泼大胆，蹦蹦跳跳地离她的脚那么近……
ex_en: A pert spirit may sometimes annoy, but a true friend knows when to be bold and when to be still.
ex_zh: 冒失的性情有时会惹人烦，但真正的朋友知道何时该大胆，何时该安静。

def_en: Lasting through many years; recurring
def_zh: 多年生的；长久的；持久的
quote_en: The flower-bed was not quite bare. It was bare of flowers because the perennial plants had been cut down for their winter rest, but there were tall shrubs and low ones which grew together at the back of the bed... (Chapter 7)
quote_zh: 花坛并非完全光秃。它没有花是因为多年生植物被剪枝过冬了，但在花坛后面有高大的灌木和低矮的灌木长在一起……
ex_en: Like perennial roots beneath the winter soil, our best hopes wait for the warmth of spring.
ex_zh: 就像冬日泥土下的多年生根茎，我们最美好的希望在等待春天的温暖。

def_en: To confuse or perplex
quote_en: The ivy was the baffling thing. Howsoever carefully she looked she could see nothing but thickly-growing, glossy, dark green leaves. (Chapter 8)
quote_zh: 常春藤才是令人困惑的东西。无论她看得多么仔细，除了茂密生长的、光滑的深绿色叶子，什么也看不到。
ex_en: What baffles us today may become tomorrow's door, if we have patience to seek the key.
ex_zh: 今天令我们困惑的事物，若有耐心去寻找钥匙，或许会成为明天的门。

def_en: Completely puzzled or confused
quote_en: She gazed at it with a mystified expression. (Chapter 8)
quote_zh: 她带着困惑的表情盯着它看。
ex_en: When we are mystified, we stand at the threshold of learning; curiosity is the key.
ex_zh: 当我们感到困惑时，我们正站在学习的门槛上；好奇心就是钥匙。

def_en: A quavering or vibratory sound, especially a rapid alternation of sung notes
quote_en: The robin flew from his swinging spray of ivy on to the top of the wall and he opened his beak and sang a loud, lovely trill, merely to show off. (Chapter 8)
quote_zh: 知更鸟从摇曳的常春藤枝条上飞到墙头，张开嘴，唱出一声响亮而可爱的颤音，纯粹是为了炫耀。
ex_en: Every heart has its own song; finding your voice is the sweetest trill of all.
ex_zh: 每颗心都有它自己的歌；找到自己的声音就是最甜美的旋律。

def_en: A slender, coiling plant stem that attaches itself to support
quote_en: There were other trees in the garden, and one of the things which made the place look strangest and loveliest was that climbing roses had run all over them and swung down long tendrils which made light swaying curtains... (Chapter 9)
quote_zh: 花园里还有别的树，让这个地方看起来最奇特、最可爱的事物之一就是攀缘玫瑰爬满了它们，垂下长长的卷须，像轻盈摇曳的帘幕……
ex_en: Like a tendril seeking light, we reach for what lifts us, and find our way to bloom.
ex_zh: 就像寻找光明的卷须，我们追寻那些提升我们的事物，找到绽放自己的路。

def_en: A tall, rounded vase, often used as a garden ornament
quote_en: There seemed to have been grass paths here and there, and in one or two corners there were alcoves of evergreen with stone seats or tall moss-covered flower urns in them. (Chapter 9)
quote_zh: 这儿那儿似乎有草地小径，在一两个角落里，有常青藤搭建的凹室，里面有石凳或高大的、长满苔藓的花瓮。
ex_en: The empty urn awaits the flower; so too does an open heart await the gift of friendship.
ex_zh: 空瓮等待花朵；敞开的胸怀也等待着友谊的馈赠。

def_en: A recess or small room adjoining a larger one
quote_en: There seemed to have been grass paths here and there, and in one or two corners there were alcoves of evergreen with stone seats or tall moss-covered flower urns in them. (Chapter 9)
quote_zh: 这儿那儿似乎有草地小径，在一两个角落里，有常青藤搭建的凹室，里面有石凳或高大的、长满苔藓的花瓮。
ex_en: Even the grandest garden needs its quiet alcoves—places to rest and grow in peace.
ex_zh: 即使是最宏伟的花园也需要安静的角落——在那里休憩，在宁静中成长。

def_en: Greatly surprised or amazed
quote_en: The bulbs in the secret garden must have been much astonished. Such nice clear places were made round them that they had all the breathing space they wanted... (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: 秘密花园里的球茎一定非常惊讶。它们周围被清理出了如此美好、干净的地方，它们有了想要的所有呼吸空间……
ex_en: When we are astonished by kindness, we begin to believe that the world can be a garden.
ex_zh: 当我们被善意所惊讶时，我们开始相信世界可以是一座花园。

def_en: Fickle and irresponsible; given to sudden whims
quote_en: "That's like him," snapped Ben Weatherstaff. "Makin' up to th' women folk just for vanity an' flightiness." (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: "它就是这样，"本·韦瑟斯塔夫厉声说。"就为了虚荣和轻浮去讨好女人。"
ex_en: A flighty heart may chase many things, but a steady heart finds one worth keeping.
ex_zh: 轻浮的心会追逐许多事物，而坚定的心只找到一样值得珍藏的。

def_en: Charming and attractive; drawing one's interest
def_zh: 迷人的；吸引人的；讨人喜欢的
quote_en: The robin was evidently in a fascinating, bold mood. He hopped closer and closer and looked at Ben Weatherstaff more and more engagingly. (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: 知更鸟显然处于一种迷人而大胆的情绪中。它跳得越来越近，越来越迷人地看着本·韦瑟斯塔夫。
ex_en: An engaging smile may open a door, but it is kindness that invites one to stay.
ex_zh: 迷人的微笑或许能打开一扇门，但善意才能邀请人留下来。

def_en: To dare to do something risky; to undertake
quote_en: "Do they quite die? Do roses quite die when they are left to themselves?" she ventured. (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: "它们会完全死掉吗？玫瑰无人照料时会完全死掉吗？"她大胆地问道。
ex_en: To venture a question is to plant a seed; understanding grows from humble asking.
ex_zh: 大胆提问就是播下一颗种子；理解从谦逊的询问中生长。

def_en: To trim a plant by cutting away dead or overgrown branches
quote_en: "Once or twice a year I'd go an' work at 'em a bit—prune 'em an' dig about th' roots. They run wild, but they was in rich soil, so some of 'em lived." (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: "每年我会去一两次，照料它们一下——修剪修剪，松松根部的土。它们长得野，但土很肥，所以有些活了下来。"
ex_en: We prune the dead to make room for the living; so too we let go of hurt to let hope grow.
ex_zh: 我们修剪枯枝，为生机腾出空间；同样，我们也放下伤痛，让希望生长。

def_en: Relating to or affected by rheumatism, causing pain in joints
quote_en: "Not been this year. My rheumatics has made me too stiff in th' joints." (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: "今年没去过。我的风湿病让关节太僵硬了。"
ex_en: Age may stiffen the body, but a young heart keeps the spirit moving.
ex_zh: 岁月或许会让身体僵硬，但年轻的心能让精神永远前行。

def_en: Shaking or quivering slightly; nervous
quote_en: ...and quite near him were two rabbits sitting up and sniffing with tremulous noses—and actually it appeared as if they were all drawing near to watch him and listen to the strange low little call his pipe seemed to make. (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: ……离他很近的地方有两只兔子，竖着身子，用颤抖的鼻子嗅着——实际上，它们似乎都在靠近，看着他，听着他那支小笛子发出的奇特而低沉的声音。
ex_en: A tremulous step is still a step forward; courage is not the absence of fear, but moving through it.
ex_zh: 颤抖的步伐仍是前进的一步；勇气并非没有恐惧，而是穿过恐惧前行。

def_en: A small hand tool with a flat blade, used for digging and planting
quote_en: "I've got th' garden tools. There's a little spade an' rake an' a fork an' hoe. Eh! they are good 'uns. There's a trowel, too." (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: "我买了园艺工具。有一把小铲子、耙子、叉子和锄头。嘿！都是好东西。还有一把小泥铲。"
ex_en: A small trowel in willing hands can turn a barren patch into a garden.
ex_zh: 一把小铲子在一双愿意动手的手中，能让荒芜的土地变成花园。

def_en: With earnest and focused attention
quote_en: The robin listened a few seconds, intently, and then answered quite as if he were replying to a question. (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: 知更鸟专注地听了几秒钟，然后回答了，就好像在回答一个问题一样。
ex_en: To listen intently is to say, "You matter," without uttering a single word.
ex_zh: 专注地倾听，就是在不说一个字的情况下说："你很重要。"

def_en: To openly disregard or mock a rule or convention
quote_en: "He wouldn't come near thee if he didn't," answered Dickon. "Birds is rare choosers an' a robin can flout a body worse than a man." (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: "他要是不喜欢你，就不会靠近你，"迪肯回答。"鸟儿可挑剔了，知更鸟比人还会给人难堪。"
ex_en: To flout wisdom is to close the door on growth; to listen is to let the light in.
ex_zh: 藐视智慧就是关上成长之门；倾听则让光明照入。

def_en: To walk in a timid or stealthy manner, often sideways
quote_en: He so sidled and twittered and tilted as he hopped on his bush. (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: 它在灌木丛上跳来跳去，时而侧身，时而啁啾，时而歪着脑袋。
ex_en: Even the shyest heart can sidle toward friendship when it finds a gentle welcome.
ex_zh: 即使是最羞怯的心，在找到温柔的欢迎时，也能悄悄靠近友谊。

def_en: Nothing; zero
quote_en: "I'm keepin' secrets all th' time," he said. "If I couldn't keep secrets from th' other lads, secrets about foxes' cubs, an' birds' nests, an' wild things' holes, there'd be naught safe on th' moor." (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: "我一直都在保守秘密，"他说。"我要不能对别的孩子保守秘密，那些关于狐狸崽、鸟巢、野生动物的洞的秘密，那荒野上就没有什么是安全的了。"
ex_en: Trust built on naught but words will crumble; trust built on deeds will stand.
ex_zh: 仅建立在言语上的信任会崩塌；建立在行动上的信任才会屹立。

def_en: Stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion or course of action
quote_en: Mistress Mary got up from the log at once. She knew she felt contrary again, and obstinate, and she did not care at all. (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: 玛丽小姐立刻从木头上站起来。她知道她又感到别扭和固执了，而且她一点也不在乎。
ex_en: An obstinate heart may stand firm, but a wise heart knows when to bend.
ex_zh: 固执的心或许能站得稳，但智慧的心知道何时该弯腰。

def_en: In a manner showing open resistance or bold disobedience
def_zh: 挑衅地；反抗地；公然违抗地
quote_en: There was a door and Mary pushed it slowly open and they passed in together, and then Mary stood and waved her hand round defiantly. "It's this," she said. "It's a secret garden, and I'm the only one in the world who wants it to be alive." (Chapter 10)
quote_zh: 有一扇门，玛丽慢慢推开，他们一起走了进去，然后玛丽站在那里，挑衅地挥了挥手。"就是这里，"她说。"这是一座秘密花园，世界上只有我希望它活过来。"
ex_en: To stand defiantly for what is right is to plant a flag that others may one day follow.
ex_zh: 为正确的事挺身反抗，就是插下一面旗帜，他人或许终将跟随。

def_en: A simple plant that grows on rocks, walls, and trees
quote_en: He stepped over to the nearest tree—an old, old one with gray lichen all over its bark, but upholding a curtain of tangled sprays and branches. (Chapter 11)
quote_zh: 他走到最近的一棵树前——那是一棵非常古老的树，树皮上满是灰色的地衣，却支撑着由缠结的枝条织成的帘幕。
ex_en: Like lichen on an ancient tree, small joys grow slowly but cling through every storm.
ex_zh: 就像古树上的地衣，小小的快乐生长缓慢，却在每一场风暴中都紧紧相依。

def_en: Showing deep respect and awe
quote_en: Mary touched it herself in an eager, reverent way. (Chapter 11)
quote_zh: 玛丽自己也用急切而恭敬的方式摸了摸它。
ex_en: A reverent heart sees wonder in a blade of grass and finds magic in the ordinary.
ex_zh: 恭敬的心能在草叶中看见奇迹，在平凡中发现魔力。

def_en: To grow or develop well; to prosper
quote_en: "They've run wild," he said, "but th' strongest ones has fair thrived on it." (Chapter 11)
quote_zh: "它们长野了，"他说，"但最强壮的那些倒是长得相当好。"
ex_en: Where love and care are given, even wild things thrive and bloom.
ex_zh: 在有爱和照料的地方，即使是野生的东西也能茁壮成长，绽放花朵。

def_en: Triumphantly joyful; jubilant
quote_en: "There!" he said exultantly. "I told thee so. There's green in that wood yet. Look at it." (Chapter 11)
quote_zh: "你看！"他得意地说。"我告诉过你吧。那木头里还有绿意。看看吧。"
ex_en: An exultant heart sees hope where others see only dust and dry branches.
ex_zh: 欢欣鼓舞的心能在别人只看到尘土和枯枝的地方看见希望。

def_en: Diligent and hard-working
quote_en: They were working industriously round one of the biggest standard roses when he caught sight of something which made him utter an exclamation of surprise. (Chapter 11)
quote_zh: 他们正在一棵最大的标准玫瑰周围勤奋地干活，这时他看到了什么东西，发出一声惊叫。
ex_en: The industrious hand, like the patient root, finds its reward in time.
ex_zh: 勤劳的手，就像耐心的根，终会得到时间的回报。

def_en: Showing malicious ill will; deliberately hurtful
quote_en: She frowned a little and gave her trowel a rather spiteful dig into the earth. (Chapter 11)
quote_zh: 她微微皱眉，有点恶意地把小铲子往土里戳了一下。
ex_en: A spiteful word wounds, but a kind one heals; choose the tool that builds, not breaks.
ex_zh: 恶意的言语伤人，而善意的言语治愈；选择建造而非破坏的工具。

def_en: To suppress or restrain; to smother
quote_en: Dickon laughed so that he was obliged to stifle the sound by putting his arm over his mouth. (Chapter 11)
quote_zh: 迪肯笑得不得不用胳膊捂住嘴，把声音压下去。
ex_en: To stifle joy is to close the window on sunshine; let laughter out and light will follow.
ex_zh: 压抑快乐就像关上阳光的窗户；让笑声出来，光明就会随之而来。

def_en: Warm and enthusiastic; robust and healthy
quote_en: "Eh!" he answered heartily, "that I does. I likes thee wonderful, an' so does th' robin, I do believe!" (Chapter 11)
quote_zh: "哎！"他热诚地回答，"我当然喜欢你。我觉得你特别好，我相信知更鸟也是！"
ex_en: A hearty welcome costs nothing but opens the door to everything.
ex_zh: 热诚的欢迎不费分文，却能打开一切的大门。

def_en: Feeling or expressing sorrow; sad
quote_en: She could not go to sleep again. The mournful sound kept her awake because she felt mournful herself. (Chapter 12)
quote_zh: 她再也睡不着了。那哀伤的声音让她无法入睡，因为她自己也感到悲伤。
ex_en: A mournful heart may feel alone, but the dawn always comes to those who wait.
ex_zh: 悲伤的心或许感到孤独，但黎明总会为等待的人到来。

def_en: Rough in texture; crude or unrefined
quote_en: He picked up his coat from the grass and brought out of a pocket a lumpy little bundle tied up in a quiet clean, coarse, blue and white handkerchief. (Chapter 11)
quote_zh: 他从草地上拿起外套，从口袋里掏出一个鼓鼓囊囊的小包裹，包在一块干净朴素的蓝白相间的粗手帕里。
ex_en: A coarse cloth may wrap the simplest bread, but kindness makes it a feast.
ex_zh: 粗糙的布或许包裹着最简单的面包，但善意让它成为盛宴。

def_en: To swell or expand from internal pressure
quote_en: His poppy-colored cheeks were distended with his first big bite of bread and bacon, but he managed to smile encouragingly. (Chapter 11)
quote_zh: 他那罂粟花般红润的脸颊因咬了一大口面包和培根而鼓了起来，但他还是努力露出了鼓励的微笑。
ex_en: A heart distended with joy has room for nothing but gratitude.
ex_zh: 被喜悦充盈的心，除了感恩再无其他空间。

def_en: Irritable and grumpy; difficult to deal with
quote_en: "If I was you, I'd ask Ben Weatherstaff," advised Martha. "He's not half as bad as he looks, for all he's so crabbed." (Chapter 12)
quote_zh: "我要是你，就去问本·韦瑟斯塔夫，"玛莎建议道。"他虽然脾气暴躁，但远没有看上去那么坏。"
ex_en: Beneath a crabbed shell may live a heart that remembers beauty and tends it still.
ex_zh: 在暴躁的外壳下，或许藏着一颗记得美丽并仍在照料它的心。

def_en: An ornament fastened with a pin or clasp
quote_en: She had on her best black dress and cap, and her collar was fastened with a large brooch with a picture of a man's face on it. (Chapter 12)
quote_zh: 她穿着最好的黑裙子和帽子，领口别着一枚大胸针，上面有一张男人的照片。
ex_en: Small treasures hold big memories; a brooch may be a key to a lifetime of love.
ex_zh: 小小的珍宝承载着大大的记忆；一枚胸针或许就是打开一生爱的钥匙。

def_en: In a very unhappy or unfortunate state; miserable
def_zh: 极不愉快的；可怜的；悲惨的
quote_en: He was not ugly. His face would have been handsome if it had not been so miserable. He looked as if the sight of her worried and fretted him and as if he did not know what in the world to do with her... "I am too ill, and wretched and distracted; but I wish you to be happy and comfortable." (Chapter 12)
quote_zh: 他并不丑。如果不是那么痛苦，他的脸本应是英俊的。他看起来像是看到她让他担忧和烦恼，不知道该怎么对她才好……"我病得太重，又痛苦又心烦意乱；但我希望你能快乐舒适。"
ex_en: Even a wretched heart can wish for another's happiness—and in that wish, begin to heal.
ex_zh: 即使是一颗痛苦的心，也能为他人祈求幸福——而在这祈愿中，它开始愈合。

def_en: To speak in a trembling or unsteady voice
quote_en: "Might I," quavered Mary, "might I have a bit of earth?" (Chapter 12)
quote_zh: "我能，"玛丽声音发颤地说，"我能要一小块土地吗？"
ex_en: A quavering voice may ask for the smallest thing, but that asking is the seed of a great garden.
ex_zh: 颤抖的声音或许只请求最小的事物，但这个请求正是一座大花园的种子。

def_en: In a formal, serious, and dignified manner
quote_en: "Martha," said Mary solemnly, "he is really a nice man, only his face is so miserable and his forehead is all drawn together." (Chapter 12)
quote_zh: "玛莎，"玛丽严肃地说，"他真是个好人，只是他的脸看起来那么痛苦，额头都皱在一起了。"
ex_en: To speak solemnly of simple truth is to honor both the words and the one who hears them.
ex_zh: 庄严地讲述简单的真理，是对言语和倾听者的尊重。

def_en: To climb or move awkwardly using hands and feet
quote_en: She went on about the roses which might have clambered from tree to tree and hung down—about the many birds which might have built their nests there because it was so safe. (Chapter 13)
quote_zh: 她又说起那些可能从一棵树攀爬到另一棵树、垂挂下来的玫瑰——还有那些可能因为那里安全而在那里筑巢的鸟儿。
ex_en: Hope clambes over walls and through tangles, finding its way to light.
ex_zh: 希望攀过高墙，穿过荆棘，找到通往光明的路。

def_en: Feeling troubled or nervous; disturbed
quote_en: "I don't know what to do!" cried agitated Martha. "If Mrs. Medlock finds out, she'll think I broke orders and told thee and I shall be packed back to mother." (Chapter 14)
quote_zh: "我不知道该怎么办！"焦虑的玛莎叫道。"要是梅德洛克太太发现了，她会以为我违反命令告诉了你，我会被打发回妈妈那里去的。"
ex_en: An agitated mind finds peace in stillness; a breath, a pause, a garden path.
ex_zh: 焦虑的心在宁静中找到平静；一次呼吸，一次停顿，一条花园小径。

def_en: A serious infectious disease causing fever and intestinal problems
quote_en: Once he had rheumatic fever an' once he had typhoid. Eh! Mrs. Medlock did get a fright then. (Chapter 14)
quote_zh: 他得过一次风湿热，还有一次得了伤寒。哎！梅德洛克太太当时可吓坏了。
ex_en: Sickness may come like a storm, but the body, like a garden, has a hidden strength to heal.
ex_zh: 疾病或许像风暴一样袭来，但身体，如同花园，有着隐藏的治愈力量。

def_en: Expressing disapproval or disappointment
quote_en: Dr. Craven turned reproachfully to Mrs. Medlock. (Chapter 14)
quote_zh: 克雷文医生不满地转向梅德洛克太太。
ex_en: A reproachful glance may sting, but a forgiving heart heals what words cannot.
ex_zh: 责备的目光或许刺痛，但宽容的心能治愈言语无法企及之处。

def_en: A person made weak or disabled by illness
quote_en: When he was amused and interested she thought he scarcely looked like an invalid at all, except that his face was so colorless and he was always on the sofa. (Chapter 15)
quote_zh: 当他开心和感兴趣的时候，她觉得他看起来根本不像个病人，只是脸色那么苍白，而且总是躺在沙发上。
ex_en: No one is truly an invalid who still has the will to grow, to dream, to reach for the sun.
ex_zh: 只要还有成长的意愿、梦想的力量、追寻阳光的渴望，就没有人是真正的弱者。

def_en: To spread out from a rolled or folded state
quote_en: There were things sprouting and pushing out from the roots of clumps of plants and there were actually here and there glimpses of royal purple and yellow unfurling among the stems of crocuses. (Chapter 15)
quote_zh: 从一丛丛植物的根部有新芽冒出，实际上，在番红花的茎间，隐约可见皇室的紫色和黄色正在展开。
ex_en: Like a leaf unfurling to the sun, each day offers us a chance to open a little more.
ex_zh: 就像向太阳展开的叶子，每一天都给我们机会，多展开一点点。

def_en: A bird's feathers
quote_en: When she had reached the place where the door hid itself under the ivy, she was startled by a curious loud sound. It was the caw—caw of a crow and it came from the top of the wall, and when she looked up, there sat a big glossy-plumaged blue-black bird, looking down at her very wisely indeed. (Chapter 15)
quote_zh: 当她走到门隐藏在常春藤下的地方时，一声奇怪而响亮的声音吓了她一跳。那是乌鸦的"嘎——嘎"声，从墙头传来，她抬头一看，一只羽毛光滑的蓝黑色大鸟坐在那里，确实非常智慧地俯视着她。
ex_en: Fine plumage may catch the eye, but it is a kind heart that makes a creature beautiful.
ex_zh: 漂亮的羽毛或许能吸引眼球，但善良的心才让生灵美丽。

def_en: To make untidy; to rumple
quote_en: He got up himself, laughing and glowing, and tousled; his eyes like a bit of the sky. (Chapter 15)
quote_zh: 他自己站了起来，笑着，满脸红光，头发乱蓬蓬的；他的眼睛像一片天空。
ex_en: A tousled head and a laughing heart are signs of a day well spent.
ex_zh: 蓬乱的头发和欢笑的内心，是度过美好一天的标志。

def_en: A feeling of intense pleasure or joy
quote_en: They put their eager young noses close to the earth and sniffed its warmed springtime breathing; they dug and pulled and laughed low with rapture until Mistress Mary's hair was as tumbled as Dickon's and her cheeks were almost as poppy red as his. (Chapter 15)
quote_zh: 他们把急切的小鼻子贴近泥土，嗅着它温暖的春天气息；他们挖着、拔着，压抑着狂喜低声笑着，直到玛丽小姐的头发变得和迪肯的一样蓬乱，脸颊也几乎和他一样红如罂粟。
ex_en: In the rapture of discovery, even the simplest soil becomes a kingdom.
ex_zh: 在发现的狂喜中，即使是最简单的泥土也变成了王国。

def_en: An idea, belief, or opinion
quote_en: "Us mustn't seem as if us was watchin' him too close," said Dickon. "He'd be out with us for good if he got th' notion us was interferin' now." (Chapter 15)
quote_zh: "咱们不能显得像是在太近地盯着它看，"迪肯说。"要是它觉得咱们现在在打扰它，就再也不会跟咱们待在一起了。"
ex_en: A single notion, planted like a seed, can grow into a forest of change.
ex_zh: 一个念头，像种子一样种下，就能长成一片改变的森林。

def_en: Expressing sorrow; mournful
quote_en: She ran back to the house as quickly as her feet would carry her. She wanted to tell Colin about Dickon's fox cub and the rook and about what the springtime had been doing... So it was not very pleasant when she opened the door of her room, to see Martha standing waiting for her with a doleful face. (Chapter 16)
quote_zh: 她以最快的速度跑回房子。她想告诉科林关于迪肯的小狐狸崽和白嘴鸦的事，还有春天在做什么……所以，当她打开房门，看到玛莎站在那里，一脸忧愁地等着她时，感觉可不怎么好。
ex_en: A doleful face may hide a hopeful heart; sometimes sorrow is just waiting for a story.
ex_zh: 忧愁的脸或许藏着一颗充满希望的心；有时悲伤只是在等待一个故事。

def_en: To do something in a haughty way; to lower oneself
quote_en: Colin frowned and condescended to look at her. (Chapter 16)
quote_zh: 科林皱了皱眉，屈尊地看了她一眼。
ex_en: Those who condescend to others build walls; those who kneel to help build bridges.
ex_zh: 屈尊俯就的人建造高墙；跪下帮助的人架起桥梁。

def_en: To stare in an angry or fierce way
quote_en: They were a nice agreeable pair as they glared at each other. (Chapter 16)
quote_zh: 他们怒视着对方，真是般配的一对。
ex_en: A glare may win a battle, but a kind word wins a friend for a lifetime.
ex_zh: 怒视或许能赢得一场争斗，但善意的话语能赢得一生的朋友。

def_en: To deny or oppose what someone has said
quote_en: "You're not!" contradicted Mary unsympathetically. (Chapter 16)
quote_zh: "你不是！"玛丽不客气地反驳道。
ex_en: To contradict may clear the air, but to listen opens the door to understanding.
ex_zh: 反驳或许能澄清事实，但倾听才能打开理解之门。

def_en: To trouble or afflict; to be ill
quote_en: "Hysterics and temper are half what ails him." (Chapter 16)
quote_zh: "歇斯底里和坏脾气占了他毛病的一半。"
ex_en: What ails the body is soon mended; what ails the spirit needs the sun of kindness.
ex_zh: 身体的病痛很快能痊愈；精神的困扰需要善意的阳光。

def_en: Not yielding in strength or determination; harsh
quote_en: She would never tell him and he could stay in his room and never get any fresh air and die if he liked! It would serve him right! She felt so sour and unrelenting that for a few minutes she almost forgot about Dickon and the green veil creeping over the world and the soft wind blowing down from the moor. (Chapter 16)
quote_zh: 她永远不会告诉他，他爱待在他房间里就待着，永远不呼吸新鲜空气，爱死就死吧！活该！她感到如此刻薄和无情，以至于有几分钟几乎忘记了迪肯、蔓延到世界上的绿色面纱，以及从荒野吹来的温柔的风。
ex_en: An unrelenting heart may win its way, but a flexible one wins the love that makes the way worth traveling.
ex_zh: 无情的心或许能赢得胜利，但柔软的心能赢得让这条路值得行走的爱。

def_en: To twist or squirm in pain or discomfort
quote_en: "I felt the lump—I felt it," choked out Colin. "I knew I should. I shall have a hunch on my back and then I shall die," and he began to writhe again and turned on his face and sobbed and wailed but he didn't scream. (Chapter 17)
quote_zh: "我摸到那个肿块了——我摸到了，"科林哽咽着说。"我就知道会有。我的背上会长出驼背，然后我就会死。"他又开始扭动起来，翻过身去，抽泣哀号着，但没有尖叫。
ex_en: Even when fear makes us writhe, a steady hand and a calm voice can call us back to hope.
ex_zh: 即使在恐惧让我们痛苦挣扎时，一只稳定的手和冷静的声音也能将我们唤回希望。

def_en: To make partial changes; to alter
quote_en: When he was very much interested he often spoke quite broad Yorkshire though at other times he tried to modify his dialect so that Mary could better understand. (Chapter 18)
quote_zh: 当他非常感兴趣时，他常常说很浓重的约克郡方言，尽管其他时候他试图改变他的口音，以便玛丽能更好地理解。
ex_en: We modify our ways not to hide who we are, but to open ourselves to those who are different.
ex_zh: 我们改变自己的方式不是为了隐藏自己，而是向那些与我们不同的人敞开心扉。

def_en: To rub or push gently with the nose or face
quote_en: He walked over to Colin's sofa and put the new-born lamb quietly on his lap, and immediately the little creature turned to the warm velvet dressing-gown and began to nuzzle and nuzzle into its folds and butt its tight-curled head with soft impatience against his side. (Chapter 19)
quote_zh: 他走到科林的沙发前，轻轻地把新生的小羊放在他的膝上，小家伙立刻转向温暖的天鹅绒晨衣，开始用鼻子在衣褶里蹭啊蹭，用柔软的、不耐烦的动作把卷曲的小脑袋抵在他身上。
ex_en: To nuzzle close to another is to say, without words, "I trust you."
ex_zh: 用鼻尖蹭着靠近，是在无言地说："我信任你。"

def_en: Lean, tough, and strong
quote_en: He was a tiny little shaggy moor pony with thick locks hanging over his eyes and with a pretty face and a nuzzling velvet nose. He was rather thin with living on moor grass but he was as tough and wiry as if the muscle in his little legs had been made of steel springs. (Chapter 18)
quote_zh: 那是一匹小小的、毛茸茸的荒野小马，厚厚的鬃毛垂在眼前，脸很漂亮，鼻子是毛茸茸的天鹅绒。靠吃荒野的草为生，它相当瘦，但却坚韧结实，仿佛它小腿上的肌肉是用钢弹簧做的。
ex_en: A wiry spirit may seem small, but it holds the strength to climb the highest hill.
ex_zh: 坚韧的精神或许看起来渺小，却蕴含着攀登最高山峰的力量。

def_en: To make the gentle, high-pitched sound of a horse
quote_en: He had lifted his head and whinnied softly the moment he saw Dickon and he had trotted up to him and put his head across his shoulder and then Dickon had talked into his ear and Jump had talked back in odd little whinnies and puffs and snorts. (Chapter 18)
quote_zh: 他一看到迪肯就抬起头，轻轻地嘶鸣着，小跑到他身边，把头搭在他肩上，然后迪肯在它耳边低语，"蹦蹦"也用奇怪的轻声嘶鸣、喷气和响鼻回应。
ex_en: A whinny of greeting needs no translation; friendship speaks in every language.
ex_zh: 问候的嘶鸣无需翻译；友谊用每一种语言说话。

def_en: The nose and mouth of an animal
quote_en: Dickon had made him give Mary his small front hoof and kiss her on her cheek with his velvet muzzle. (Chapter 18)
quote_zh: 迪肯让它抬起小小的前蹄给玛丽，还用毛绒绒的鼻口部亲了亲她的脸颊。
ex_en: A velvet muzzle can teach what words cannot: that trust is the truest language.
ex_zh: 毛绒绒的鼻口部能教会言语无法教授的事：信任是最真实的语言。

def_en: Artificial behavior meant to impress; pretension
quote_en: "Yes," answered Mary without any affectation. "I should have detested you if I had seen you before I saw the robin and Dickon." (Chapter 18)
quote_zh: "是的，"玛丽毫不做作地回答。"要是我在看到知更鸟和迪肯之前见到你，我会讨厌你的。"
ex_en: Let there be no affectation between true friends; honesty is the soil where trust grows.
ex_zh: 真正的朋友之间不应有做作；诚实是信任生长的土壤。

def_en: The act of giving in to desires; leniency
quote_en: "How is he?" he asked Mrs. Medlock rather irritably when he arrived. "He will break a blood-vessel in one of those fits some day. The boy is half insane with hysteria and self-indulgence." (Chapter 19)
quote_zh: "他怎么样？"他到达时相当恼怒地问梅德洛克太太。"总有一天他会在那种发作中血管破裂。这孩子一半是被歇斯底里和自我放纵逼疯的。"
ex_en: Too much indulgence weakens; the right balance of kindness and firmness makes us strong.
ex_zh: 过度的放纵会削弱人；善意与坚定恰到好处的平衡才能让人强大。

def_en: Having no moral principles; dishonest
quote_en: Dr. Craven felt rather alarmed. If this tiresome hysterical boy should chance to get well he himself would lose all chance of inheriting Misselthwaite; but he was not an unscrupulous man, though he was a weak one, and he did not intend to let him run into actual danger. (Chapter 19)
quote_zh: 克雷文医生感到相当警觉。如果这个烦人的歇斯底里的男孩碰巧好了，他自己就会失去继承米瑟斯韦特庄园的所有机会；但他不是一个不择手段的人，尽管他很软弱，他并不打算让他陷入真正的危险。
ex_en: An unscrupulous path may seem swift, but it leads to a lonely summit.
ex_zh: 不择手段的道路或许看似快捷，却通往孤独的顶峰。

def_en: In a fluent, talkative manner
quote_en: Mrs. Medlock smiled. She was fond of Susan Sowerby. "She's got a way with her, has Susan," she went on quite volubly. (Chapter 19)
quote_zh: 梅德洛克太太笑了。她喜欢苏珊·索尔比。"她有一套本事，苏珊，"她相当健谈地继续说。
ex_en: Words spoken volubly may amuse, but words spoken from the heart stay with us.
ex_zh: 滔滔不绝的话语或许能逗乐，但发自内心的话语会留在我们心中。

def_en: Having sharp judgment; astute
quote_en: "She's a shrewd woman," said Dr. Craven, putting on his coat. (Chapter 19)
quote_zh: "她是个精明的女人，"克雷文医生边说边穿上外套。
ex_en: A shrewd mind sees the path; a kind heart chooses the right one.
ex_zh: 精明的头脑能看清道路；善良的心能选择正确的路。

def_en: A person who lives a solitary life and avoids others
quote_en: She found the servants' hall a more amusing place than the invalid's chamber and just now everybody wanted to hear the news from up-stairs. There was a great deal of joking about the unpopular young recluse who, as the cook said, "had found his master, and good for him." (Chapter 19)
quote_zh: 她发现仆人厅比病人的房间有趣得多，这会儿每个人都想听楼上的消息。大家开了很多玩笑，说那个不受欢迎的年轻隐士，正如厨娘所说，"找到了能治他的人，对他有好处。"
ex_en: A recluse may hide from the world, but a friend brings the world to his door.
ex_zh: 隐士或许躲避世界，但朋友会把世界带到他的门前。

def_en: Severe or strict in manner; having no comforts
quote_en: "I told you he was a charmer," said Colin austerely. "Charmers' animals never bite." (Chapter 19)
quote_zh: "我告诉过你他是个有魔力的人，"科林严厉地说。"有魔力的人的动物从不咬人。"
ex_en: An austere word may command attention, but a gentle one wins the heart.
ex_zh: 严厉的话语或许能引起注意，但温柔的话语能赢得人心。

def_en: To bury or drown beneath a mass; to overpower
quote_en: Colin had never talked to a boy in his life and he was so overwhelmed by his own pleasure and curiosity that he did not even think of speaking. (Chapter 19)
quote_zh: 科林这辈子从未和男孩说过话，他被自己的愉悦和好奇心深深淹没，甚至没想到要开口。
ex_en: To be overwhelmed by wonder is to stand at the door of a new world.
ex_zh: 被惊奇所淹没，就是站在一个新世界的门口。

def_en: Extremely hungry; voracious
quote_en: He pushed the rubber tip of the bottle into the nuzzling mouth and the lamb began to suck it with ravenous ecstasy. (Chapter 19)
quote_zh: 他把奶瓶的橡胶嘴塞进那蹭来蹭去的小嘴里，小羊开始贪婪而狂喜地吮吸起来。
ex_en: A ravenous hunger for life is the first sign that the spirit is waking from its sleep.
ex_zh: 对生命贪婪的渴望，是精神从沉睡中苏醒的第一个迹象。

def_en: The weak, wavering cry of a sheep, goat, or calf
quote_en: "Listen again! Do you hear a bleat—a tiny one?" (Chapter 19)
quote_zh: "再听！你听到咩咩声了吗——很细小的那种？"
ex_en: The smallest bleat can find an answer when there is an ear ready to listen.
ex_zh: 最微弱的咩咩声，只要有愿意倾听的耳朵，就能得到回应。

def_en: A short journey or trip, often for pleasure
quote_en: While he talked, Soot flew solemnly in and out of the open window and cawed remarks about the scenery while Nut and Shell made excursions into the big trees outside and ran up and down trunks and explored branches. (Chapter 19)
quote_zh: 他说话的时候，"煤灰"庄严地在开着的窗户里飞进飞出，对风景发出"嘎嘎"的评论，而"坚果"和"贝壳"则跑到外面的大树上探险，在树干上爬上爬下，探索着树枝。
ex_en: Every small excursion is a step toward discovery; every discovery, a step toward growing.
ex_zh: 每一次小小的远足都是走向发现的一步；每一次发现，都是走向成长的一步。

def_en: Based on reason; sensible
quote_en: They would go up this path and down that one and cross the other and go round among the fountain flower-beds as if they were looking at the "bedding-out plants" the head gardener, Mr. Roach, had been having arranged. That would seem such a rational thing to do that no one would think it at all mysterious. (Chapter 20)
quote_zh: 他们会走上这条路，再走下那条路，穿过另一条，在喷泉花坛间绕行，就像在观赏园丁长罗奇先生让人布置的"花坛植物"一样。这看起来会是件非常合理的事，没有人会觉得神秘。
ex_en: What seems rational on the surface may hide the most wonderful secrets; trust the heart as well as the head.
ex_zh: 表面上看起来合理的事情，可能隐藏着最美妙的秘密；既要相信头脑，也要相信内心。

def_en: Involving many carefully arranged details
quote_en: It was almost as serious and elaborately thought out as the plans of march made by great generals in time of war. (Chapter 20)
quote_zh: 这几乎像战争时期伟大将军制定的行军计划一样严肃，经过精心策划。
ex_en: An elaborate plan may succeed, but the simplest kindness often achieves the most.
ex_zh: 精心策划的计划或许能成功，但最简单的善意往往成就最多。

def_en: Without being affected by; in spite of
quote_en: Rumors of the new and curious things which were occurring in the invalid's apartments had of course filtered through the servants' hall into the stable yards and out among the gardeners, but notwithstanding this, Mr. Roach was startled one day when he received orders from Master Colin's room... (Chapter 20)
quote_zh: 病人房间里发生的那些新奇事物的传闻，当然已经通过仆人厅传到了马厩，又传到了园丁们中间，尽管如此，有一天罗奇先生接到科林少爷房间传来的命令时还是吃了一惊……
ex_en: Notwithstanding the whispers of others, the truth will stand when it is built on love.
ex_zh: 尽管他人议论纷纷，建立在爱之上的真相终将屹立。

def_en: Strange or mysterious, especially in an unsettling way
def_zh: 离奇的；神秘的；超乎寻常的
quote_en: He had never caught even a glimpse of the boy and had heard a dozen exaggerated stories about his uncanny looks and ways and his insane tempers. (Chapter 20)
quote_zh: 他甚至从未瞥见过那个男孩，却听过十几个关于他离奇外表、举止和疯狂脾气的夸张故事。
ex_en: What seems uncanny is often simply what we have not yet learned to understand.
ex_zh: 看似离奇的事物，往往只是我们尚未学会理解的事物。

def_en: Overly imaginative; unreal
def_zh: 想象的；奇异的；异想天开的
quote_en: The thing he had heard oftenest was that he might die at any moment and there had been numerous fanciful descriptions of a humped back and helpless limbs, given by people who had never seen him. (Chapter 20)
quote_zh: 他最常听到的是他随时可能死去，还有许多从未见过他的人的奇思妙想，描述他驼背、四肢无力。
ex_en: Fanciful tales may spread like weeds, but truth grows like a rose—slowly, but with real roots.
ex_zh: 异想天开的故事可能像野草一样蔓延，但真相像玫瑰一样生长——缓慢，却扎根真实。

def_en: Until this time; previously
quote_en: "Things are changing in this house, Mr. Roach," said Mrs. Medlock, as she led him up the back staircase to the corridor on to which opened the hitherto mysterious chamber. (Chapter 20)
quote_zh: "这栋房子里正在发生变化，罗奇先生，"梅德洛克太太边说边领他走上后楼梯，来到那条通往至今仍神秘的房间的走廊。
ex_en: What was hitherto hidden may become the greatest treasure when we dare to open the door.
ex_zh: 当我们敢于打开门时，那些直到现在都隐藏着的事物，可能会成为最宝贵的财富。

def_en: A collection of wild animals kept for exhibition
quote_en: "Don't you be surprised, Mr. Roach, if you find yourself in the middle of a menagerie and Martha Sowerby's Dickon more at home than you or me could ever be." (Chapter 20)
quote_zh: "罗奇先生，要是你发现自己置身于一个小动物园，而玛莎·索尔比的迪肯比你我更自在，你可别吃惊。"
ex_en: A heart that loves all creatures makes a menagerie of the world, and every beast a friend.
ex_zh: 一颗热爱所有生灵的心，把世界变成了动物园，每只动物都是朋友。

def_en: Merciful or tolerant; not strict
quote_en: "He'd be at home in Buckingham Palace or at the bottom of a coal mine," he said. "And yet it's not impudence, either. He's just fine, is that lad." (Chapter 20)
quote_zh: "他在白金汉宫或煤矿井底都能自在，"他说。"而且这也不是无礼。这孩子就是好。"
ex_en: A lenient heart knows that each soul blooms in its own season; patience is the sun that warms them all.
ex_zh: 宽容的心知道，每个灵魂都在自己的季节里绽放；耐心是温暖它们所有人的阳光。

def_en: A partner or companion; a spouse
quote_en: "My word!" he said, "he's got a fine lordly way with him, hasn't he? You'd think he was a whole Royal Family rolled into one—Prince Consort and all." (Chapter 20)
quote_zh: "天哪！"他说，"他可真有派头，是不是？你会以为他是整个王室合而为一——连亲王配偶都在内。"
ex_en: A true consort walks beside, not ahead; the journey is sweeter when taken together.
ex_zh: 真正的伴侣并肩而行，而非走在前面；共同走过的旅途更加甜美。

def_en: Characterized by an unhealthy interest in disturbing subjects
quote_en: Shut in and morbid as his life had been, Colin had more imagination than she had and at least he had spent a good deal of time looking at wonderful books and pictures. (Chapter 20)
quote_zh: 尽管他的生活封闭而病态，科林的想象力却比她丰富，至少他花了很多时间看精彩的书和画。
ex_en: A morbid mind dwells on shadows; a hopeful one turns toward the light and finds it.
ex_zh: 病态的头脑沉溺于阴影；充满希望的头脑转向光明并找到它。

def_en: A line of people or vehicles moving in an orderly way
quote_en: "That morning when you ran in and said 'It's come! It's come!' you made me feel quite queer. It sounded as if things were coming with a great procession and big bursts and wafts of music." (Chapter 20)
quote_zh: "那天早上你跑进来说'它来了！它来了！'让我感觉很奇怪。听起来好像有什么东西随着盛大的队伍来了，伴随着迸发的音乐和飘荡的旋律。"
ex_en: Life's joys arrive in quiet procession; we need only open our eyes to see them pass.
ex_zh: 生活中的喜悦以安静的行列到来；我们只需睁开眼睛就能看到它们经过。

def_en: To become red or rosy; to blush
quote_en: Colin flushed tremendously. "Walk!" he said. "Dig! Shall I?" (Chapter 20)
quote_zh: 科林脸涨得通红。"走路！"他说。"挖土！我可以吗？"
ex_en: A flush of hope is the first color of a new beginning.
ex_zh: 希望的红晕是崭新开始的第一抹色彩。

def_en: To rule or prevail; to be widespread
quote_en: And delight reigned. (Chapter 21)
quote_zh: 喜悦弥漫着。
ex_en: When delight reigns in the heart, the whole world becomes a garden.
ex_zh: 当喜悦在心中占据统治地位时，整个世界都变成了花园。

def_en: A covering that shelters or shades
quote_en: They drew the chair under the plum-tree, which was snow-white with blossoms and musical with bees. It was like a king's canopy, a fairy king's. (Chapter 21)
quote_zh: 他们把椅子拉到李子树下，树上开满了雪白的花朵，蜜蜂在嗡嗡作响。就像国王的天篷，一位仙王的。
ex_en: The simplest tree becomes a royal canopy when shared with a friend.
ex_zh: 当与朋友分享时，最普通的树也成了王室的天篷。

def_en: To make nervous or confused
quote_en: Tha'll see him flyin' backward an' for'ard carryin' worms nigh as big as himsel' an' that much noise goin' on in th' nest when he gets there as fair flusters him so as he scarce knows which big mouth to drop th' first piece in. (Chapter 21)
quote_zh: 你会看到它飞来飞去，叼着和自己差不多大的虫子，等它回到巢里，里面那么吵，把它弄得晕头转向，几乎不知道该先把虫子塞进哪张大嘴里。
ex_en: Even the busiest bird may fluster, but love finds a way to feed every hungry mouth.
ex_zh: 即使是最忙碌的鸟儿也会慌张，但爱总能找到喂养每一张饥饿小嘴的方法。

def_en: To search for food or provisions
quote_en: "Eh!" with a sudden relieved start and laying his hand on Colin. "Look at that robin! There he is! He's been foragin' for his mate." (Chapter 21)
quote_zh: "哎！"他突然松了口气，把手放在科林身上。"看那只知更鸟！它在那儿！一直在为它的伴侣觅食呢。"
ex_en: To forage for another is to say, "What is mine is yours, and we will not go hungry."
ex_zh: 为他人觅食，是在说："我的就是你的，我们不会挨饿。"

def_en: To lecture or address in a forceful, critical way
quote_en: "I never thowt much o' thee!" he harangued. "I couldna' abide thee th' first time I set eyes on thee." (Chapter 21)
quote_zh: "我从来就没觉得你好！"他大声训斥道。"我第一次见到你就受不了你。"
ex_en: A harangue may vent anger, but a quiet word can mend what harshness breaks.
ex_zh: 大声训斥或许能发泄愤怒，但安静的话语能修复粗暴所破坏的。

def_en: To signal or summon with a gesture
quote_en: At the first sound of his torrent of words Colin had been so surprised that he had only sat up and listened as if he were spellbound. But in the midst of it he had recovered himself and beckoned imperiously to Dickon. (Chapter 21)
quote_zh: 听到他那连珠炮似的话语，科林一开始惊讶得只是坐起来听着，仿佛被迷住了。但在那中间，他回过神来，傲慢地向迪肯招手。
ex_en: The future beckons to those brave enough to answer; each step toward it is a step into wonder.
ex_zh: 未来向那些足够勇敢回应的人招手；迈向它的每一步，都是步入奇迹的一步。

def_en: Knotted and twisted, often from age
quote_en: Ben Weatherstaff put his gnarled hand up and passed it over his eyes and over his forehead and then he did answer in a queer shaky voice. (Chapter 21)
quote_zh: 本·韦瑟斯塔夫举起他那双粗糙多节的手，擦了擦眼睛和前额，然后用一种奇怪而颤抖的声音回答。
ex_en: A gnarled hand may hold more wisdom than a young one; age writes stories in every line.
ex_zh: 粗糙多节的手或许比年轻的手蕴含着更多智慧；岁月在每一条纹路里书写着故事。

def_en: Lacking sensitivity or skill in dealing with others
quote_en: He was an ignorant old man and a tactless old man and he could only remember the things he had heard. (Chapter 21)
quote_zh: 他是个无知的老头，也是个不懂人情世故的老头，只能记住他听说的那些事。
ex_en: Even the tactless may speak truth; the wise listen for the truth beneath the rough words.
ex_zh: 即使是不懂人情世故的人也可能说出真话；智慧的人会在粗粝的言语之下倾听真相。

def_en: A ghost or ghostlike image
quote_en: "Eh!" he burst forth, "th' lies folk tells! Tha'rt as thin as a lath an' as white as a wraith, but there's not a knob on thee. Tha'lt make a mon yet. God bless thee!" (Chapter 21)
quote_zh: "哎！"他突然喊道，"人们说的那些谎话！你瘦得像根板条，白得像鬼魂，但你身上一个疙瘩都没有。你将来会成为真正的男子汉。上帝保佑你！"
ex_en: No wraith of fear can hold us when we find the strength that lives within.
ex_zh: 当我们找到内心深处的力量时，没有恐惧的幽灵能束缚我们。

def_en: Easily irritated; impatient and irritable
quote_en: "What art sayin'?" he asked rather testily because he did not want his attention distracted from the long thin straight boy figure and proud face. (Chapter 22)
quote_zh: "你在说什么？"他有些不耐烦地问道，因为他不希望自己的注意力从那个瘦高挺拔的男孩身影和骄傲的脸庞上分散开。
ex_en: A testy word may hide a heart that cares too much to show it.
ex_zh: 一句暴躁的话语，可能藏着一颗太过在意却不知如何表达的心。

def_en: To continue steadfastly despite difficulty
quote_en: Colin persevered. After he had turned a few trowelfuls of soil he spoke exultantly to Dickon in his best Yorkshire. (Chapter 22)
quote_zh: 科林坚持着。当他翻了几铲土之后，用他最地道的约克郡方言得意地对迪肯说。
ex_en: Those who persevere through the first difficult steps will one day walk with ease.
ex_zh: 那些在最初艰难的脚步中坚持下来的人，终有一天会轻松地行走。

def_en: To walk with difficulty; to limp
quote_en: Ben Weatherstaff brought the rose in its pot from the greenhouse. He hobbled over the grass as fast as he could. (Chapter 22)
quote_zh: 本·韦瑟斯塔夫从温室里带来了种在盆里的玫瑰。他尽可能快地蹒跚着穿过草地。
ex_en: Even a hobbling step is a step; the journey of a thousand miles begins with one.
ex_zh: 即使是蹒跚的步伐也是前行；千里之行始于足下。

def_en: Treating all equally; fair and unbiased
quote_en: "Am I queer?" he demanded. "Yes," answered Mary, "very. But you needn't be cross," she added impartially, "because so am I queer—and so is Ben Weatherstaff." (Chapter 23)
quote_zh: "我古怪吗？"他问。"是的，"玛丽回答，"非常古怪。但你不用生气，"她公正地补充道，"因为我也古怪——本·韦瑟斯塔夫也是。"
ex_en: An impartial eye sees the truth; an open heart accepts it without fear.
ex_zh: 公正的眼睛看见真相；敞开的心接受它而不恐惧。

def_en: A narrow crack or opening
quote_en: At first it seemed that green things would never cease pushing their way through the earth, in the grass, in the beds, even in the crevices of the walls. (Chapter 23)
quote_zh: 起初，绿色的事物似乎永远不会停止从泥土中、草丛里、花坛里，甚至是墙壁的缝隙中探出头来。
ex_en: Life finds a way in the smallest crevice; hope grows where we least expect it.
ex_zh: 生命在最微小的缝隙里也能找到出路；希望在我们最意想不到的地方生长。

def_en: A color or shade
quote_en: Then the green things began to show buds and the buds began to unfurl and show color, every shade of blue, every shade of purple, every tint and hue of crimson. (Chapter 23)
quote_zh: 然后绿色的事物开始露出花蕾，花蕾开始展开，展现出色彩，每一种蓝色，每一种紫色，每一种深红的色调和色彩。
ex_en: Every hue of the garden reminds us that the world is richer when we embrace all its colors.
ex_zh: 花园的每一种色调都提醒我们，当我们拥抱所有的色彩时，世界更加丰富。

def_en: A mixture used to bind bricks or stones
quote_en: Ben Weatherstaff had seen it done and had himself scraped out mortar from between the bricks of the wall and made pockets of earth for lovely clinging things to grow on. (Chapter 23)
quote_zh: 本·韦瑟斯塔夫见过那番景象，他自己也曾从墙砖之间刮出灰泥，做成盛土的口袋，让那些可爱的攀缘植物生长。
ex_en: From the mortar between stones, flowers find a way; from the hardest places, beauty may rise.
ex_zh: 从石头间的灰泥里，花朵也能找到出路；从最坚硬的地方，美丽也能升起。

def_en: Immediately; without delay
quote_en: "Aye, aye, sir!" answered Ben Weatherstaff promptly, though this was the first time he had heard of great scientific discoveries. (Chapter 23)
quote_zh: "是，是，少爷！"本·韦瑟斯塔夫立刻回答，尽管这是他第一次听说伟大的科学发现。
ex_en: A promptly offered hand can catch a falling friend; kindness waits for no invitation.
ex_zh: 及时伸出的手能接住跌倒的朋友；善意无需等待邀请。

def_en: A Muslim or Hindu religious ascetic who lives solely on alms
quote_en: "The great scientific discoveries I am going to make," he went on, "will be about Magic. Magic is a great thing and scarcely any one knows anything about it except a few people in old books—and Mary a little, because she was born in India where there are fakirs." (Chapter 23)
quote_zh: "我要做出的伟大科学发现，"他继续说，"将与魔法有关。魔法是伟大的事物，除了旧书中的少数人，几乎没有人了解它——玛丽知道一点，因为她出生在印度，那里有托钵僧。"
ex_en: The fakir finds magic in stillness; we may find it in a garden, where every seed is a promise.
ex_zh: 托钵僧在静默中寻找魔法；我们或许能在花园里找到它，在那里，每一颗种子都是一个承诺。

def_en: Grand and impressive in appearance
def_zh: 壮观的；令人印象深刻的
quote_en: This sounded so imposing that Ben Weatherstaff became quite excited and really could not keep still. (Chapter 23)
quote_zh: 这话听起来如此气派，本·韦瑟斯塔夫变得相当激动，简直坐不住了。
ex_en: An imposing manner may command the room, but a gentle heart commands the soul.
ex_zh: 气派的举止或许能掌控房间，但温柔的心能掌控灵魂。

def_en: To give pleasure or satisfaction
quote_en: Ordinarily he was very fixed in being what he called "agen' prayer-meetin's" but this being the Rajah's affair he did not resent it and was indeed inclined to be gratified at being called upon to assist. (Chapter 23)
quote_zh: 通常他非常坚定地反对他所谓的"祈祷会"，但这是小王公的事，他并不反感，反而对被叫去帮忙感到有些高兴。
ex_en: To gratify another's wish is to plant a seed of joy that may bloom for both.
ex_zh: 满足他人的愿望，就是种下一颗喜悦的种子，它可能为双方绽放。

def_en: A member of a Muslim religious order known for spinning as worship
quote_en: "Now we will begin," he said. "Shall we sway backward and forward, Mary, as if we were dervishes?" (Chapter 23)
quote_zh: "现在我们要开始了，"他说。"我们要前后摇摆吗，玛丽，就像托钵僧一样？"
ex_en: Like dervishes in joy, we may spin with delight when we find ourselves truly alive.
ex_zh: 如同喜悦中的托钵僧，当我们发现自己真正活着时，可能会因快乐而旋转。

def_en: To encourage or arouse activity or growth
quote_en: And the thought which stimulated him more than any other was this imagining what his father would look like when he saw that he had a son who was as straight and strong as other fathers' sons. (Chapter 23)
quote_zh: 比其他任何想法更能激励他的是想象父亲看到他有一个和其他父亲的儿子一样挺拔强壮的儿子时会是怎样的表情。
ex_en: A hopeful thought can stimulate the spirit more than any medicine; belief is the seed of becoming.
ex_zh: 一个充满希望的想法比任何药物都更能激励精神；信念是成为的种子。

def_en: To ignore or rebuff rudely
quote_en: He really did not mind being snubbed since the snubbing meant that the lad was gaining strength and spirit. (Chapter 23)
quote_zh: 他其实并不介意被冷落，因为被冷落意味着小伙子正在获得力量和精气神。
ex_en: A snub may sting, but a heart focused on its own growth finds room only for forgiveness.
ex_zh: 冷落或许刺痛人心，但专注于自身成长的心，只容得下宽容。

def_en: To plant seeds; to scatter
quote_en: He had bought penny packages of flower seeds now and then and sown bright sweet-scented things among gooseberry bushes and even cabbages... (Chapter 24)
quote_zh: 他时不时会买几便士一包的花籽，在醋栗丛甚至卷心菜中间播撒那些鲜艳芬芳的花朵……
ex_en: Sow kindness without counting the cost; the harvest will surprise you.
ex_zh: 播撒善良，不计代价；收获会给你惊喜。

def_en: Generously given; plentiful
quote_en: "Tell her she has been most bounteous and our gratitude is extreme." (Chapter 24)
quote_zh: "告诉她她非常慷慨，我们感激不尽。"
ex_en: A bounteous heart gives not from excess, but from the joy of sharing.
ex_zh: 慷慨的心给予，不是因为拥有太多，而是因为分享的快乐。

def_en: Abundant in quantity
quote_en: And then forgetting his grandeur he fell to and stuffed himself with buns and drank milk out of the pail in copious draughts in the manner of any hungry little boy... (Chapter 24)
quote_zh: 然后他忘了自己的威严，开始大吃起来，把小圆面包塞进嘴里，像任何一个饥饿的小男孩一样，从桶里大口大口地喝着牛奶……
ex_en: Copious laughter and copious kindness are the marks of a life well lived.
ex_zh: 开怀大笑和慷慨善良，是活得精彩的标志。

def_en: Having spiritual significance; mysterious
quote_en: Every beautiful morning the Magic was worked by the mystic circle under the plum-tree which provided a canopy of thickening green leaves after its brief blossom-time was ended. (Chapter 24)
quote_zh: 每一个美丽的早晨，魔法都在李树下那神秘的圈子里施展，在短暂的花期结束后，那里有越来越茂密的绿叶形成的天篷。
ex_en: The mystic language of a garden speaks to those who pause to listen.
ex_zh: 花园神秘的语言，对那些停下来倾听的人诉说。

def_en: A slight trace of color; a small amount
quote_en: The waxen tinge had left Colin's skin and a warm rose showed through it; his beautiful eyes were clear and the hollows under them and in his cheeks and temples had filled out. (Chapter 24)
quote_zh: 科林皮肤上那蜡黄的色泽已经褪去，透出温暖的玫瑰色；他美丽的眼睛清澈明亮，眼睛下面、脸颊和太阳穴处的凹陷也丰满了。
ex_en: The first tinge of health is like the first light of dawn; it promises a new day.
ex_zh: 健康的第一抹色彩如同黎明的第一缕光；它预示着新的一天。

def_en: Strict or harsh; very serious
quote_en: Mary became quite severe in her manner. "It was something between a sneeze and a cough," she replied with reproachful dignity, "and it got into my throat." (Chapter 24)
quote_zh: 玛丽的态度变得相当严肃。"那是一种介于打喷嚏和咳嗽之间的声音，"她带着责备的尊严回答，"它卡在我嗓子里了。"
ex_en: A severe face may hide a laughing heart; wait for the smile to show the truth.
ex_zh: 严肃的面孔或许藏着一颗欢笑的心；等待微笑显露真相。

def_en: Looking or feeling sad; gloomy
quote_en: "The glummest, ill-natured little thing she used to be and now her and Master Colin laugh together like a pair of crazy young ones." (Chapter 24)
quote_zh: "她过去是最阴沉、最坏脾气的小东西，现在她和科林少爷像一对疯狂的小家伙一样一起大笑。"
ex_en: A glum face may lift when a friend appears; we all need someone to remind us of joy.
ex_zh: 当朋友出现时，阴沉的脸庞也会扬起；我们都需要有人提醒我们快乐。

def_en: Clearly different; unmistakable
quote_en: He could speak robin (which is a quite distinct language not to be mistaken for any other). (Chapter 25)
quote_zh: 他能说知更鸟的语言（这是一种非常独特的语言，不会与其他语言混淆）。
ex_en: Every heart has its own distinct song; the beauty is in learning to listen.
ex_zh: 每颗心都有自己独特的歌；美在于学会倾听。

def_en: Causing one to feel uneasy or confused
def_zh: 令人不安的；令人困惑的
quote_en: He did not act as the other humans did. He seemed very fond of walking but he had a way of sitting or lying down for a while and then getting up in a disconcerting manner to begin again. (Chapter 25)
quote_zh: 他表现得不像其他人。他似乎很喜欢走路，但有时会坐或躺一会儿，然后以一种令人困惑的方式站起来重新开始。
ex_en: What seems disconcerting at first may be simply a new way of learning to stand.
ex_zh: 起初令人困惑的事情，可能只是一种新的学习站立的方式。

def_en: Able to express oneself easily and smoothly
quote_en: ...but as the boy who could speak robin so fluently was doing the thing with them, birds could be quite sure that the actions were not of a dangerous nature. (Chapter 25)
quote_zh: ……但既然那个能流利地说知更鸟语的男孩也和他们一起做这些动作，鸟儿们完全可以确信这些动作不具有危险性。
ex_en: To become fluent in kindness is to learn the language that every heart understands.
ex_zh: 学会流利地表达善意，就是学习每颗心都能理解的语言。

def_en: To waste away or deteriorate from lack of use
quote_en: If you have to fly about to find every meal you eat, your muscles do not become atrophied (atrophied means wasted away through want of use). (Chapter 25)
quote_zh: 如果你必须飞来飞去寻找每一餐饭，你的肌肉就不会萎缩（"萎缩"的意思是因缺乏使用而退化）。
ex_en: The muscles of kindness atrophy when unused; exercise them daily, and they grow strong.
ex_zh: 善良的肌肉不用就会萎缩；每天锻炼它们，它们就会变得强壮。

def_en: Restless; impatient under restraint
quote_en: One morning when the rain streamed down unceasingly and Colin was beginning to feel a little restive, as he was obliged to remain on his sofa because it was not safe to get up and walk about, Mary had an inspiration. (Chapter 25)
quote_zh: 一天早上，雨下个不停，科林开始感到有些焦躁，因为他不得不待在沙发上，站起来走动不安全，这时玛丽有了一个主意。
ex_en: A restive spirit longs to move; give it purpose, and it becomes a force for growth.
ex_zh: 焦躁的精神渴望行动；赋予它目标，它就会成为成长的力量。

def_en: Excessive; beyond reasonable limits
def_zh: 过度的；超出合理限度的
quote_en: Mary giggled inordinately. (Chapter 25)
quote_zh: 玛丽笑得太过分了。
ex_en: An inordinate worry steals today's peace for a tomorrow that may never come.
ex_zh: 过度的忧虑会用今天的平静去换取一个可能永远不会到来的明天。

def_en: A series of words spoken as a magic spell
quote_en: Their belief in the Magic was an abiding thing. After the morning's incantations Colin sometimes gave them Magic lectures. (Chapter 26)
quote_zh: 他们对魔法的信念是持久的。在早晨的咒语之后，科林有时会给他们讲关于魔法的课。
ex_en: The truest incantation is not words, but the steady practice of hope and courage.
ex_zh: 最真实的咒语不是言语，而是对希望和勇气的坚持实践。

def_en: Having a healthy red color
quote_en: That morning Dickon was too late to hear the lecture. When he came he was ruddy with running and his funny face looked more twinkling than usual. (Chapter 26)
quote_zh: 那天早上迪肯来得太晚，没听到讲座。他来时因为奔跑而脸色红润，那张有趣的脸看起来比平时更加神采奕奕。
ex_en: A ruddy cheek and a laughing eye are the rewards of a life lived outdoors and with friends.
ex_zh: 红润的脸颊和含笑的眼睛，是户外生活与朋友相伴的奖赏。

def_en: To mark with spots or patches of color
quote_en: With the ivy behind her, the sunlight drifting through the trees and dappling her long blue cloak, and her nice fresh face smiling across the greenery she was rather like a softly colored illustration in one of Colin's books. (Chapter 26)
quote_zh: 她身后是常春藤，阳光透过树丛洒下，在她长长的蓝色斗篷上投下斑驳的光影，她那干净清新的脸庞隔着绿植微笑着，她就像科林书中一幅色彩柔和的插画。
ex_en: Sunlight dapples the forest floor, reminding us that light always finds its way through even the thickest leaves.
ex_zh: 阳光在林间洒下斑驳光影，提醒我们光线总能穿透最茂密的树叶。

def_en: A person who is excessively anxious about their health
def_zh: 疑病症患者；过分担心健康的人
quote_en: So long as Colin shut himself up in his room and thought only of his fears and weakness and his detestation of people who looked at him and reflected hourly on humps and early death, he was a hysterical half-crazy little hypochondriac who knew nothing of the sunshine and the spring... (Chapter 27)
quote_zh: 只要科林把自己关在房间里，只想着自己的恐惧和虚弱，以及他对那些看着他的人的厌恶，每时每刻都在想着驼背和早逝，他就是个歇斯底里、半疯的小疑病症患者，对阳光和春天一无所知……
ex_en: The hypochondriac dwells on what might go wrong; the healed soul runs out to meet what goes right.
ex_zh: 疑病患者沉湎于可能出错的事；治愈的灵魂奔向美好的事物。

def_en: Not flowing; inactive or sluggish
quote_en: It was as if a sweet clear spring had begun to rise in a stagnant pool and had risen and risen until at last it swept the dark water away. (Chapter 27)
quote_zh: 仿佛一股清澈甘甜的泉水开始在停滞的池塘中涌出，不断上升，直到最后冲走了黑暗的水。
ex_en: Let no stagnant thought linger; let fresh hope spring up and wash the shadows away.
ex_zh: 不要让停滞的念头停留；让新的希望涌出，冲走阴影。

def_en: A state of rest or tranquility
quote_en: "The singular calmness remained with him the rest of the evening and he slept a new reposeful sleep; but it was not with him very long." (Chapter 27)
quote_zh: 那奇特的平静在他身上持续了整个晚上，他睡了一个新的、安宁的觉；但它没有持续太久。
ex_en: True repose is not the absence of effort, but the peace of knowing you are growing.
ex_zh: 真正的宁静并非没有努力，而是知道自己在成长的那份平静。

def_en: Lush green vegetation
quote_en: There he found the loveliness of a dream. He spent his days upon the crystal blueness of the lake or he walked back into the soft thick verdure of the hills and tramped until he was tired so that he might sleep. (Chapter 27)
quote_zh: 在那里，他找到了梦中的美好。他在水晶般蓝色的湖面上度过时光，或者走回山上柔软茂密的草木中，徒步直到疲惫，这样才能入睡。
ex_en: The verdure of the hills whispers a secret: life renews itself when we let it.
ex_zh: 山间葱郁的草木低语着一个秘密：当我们放手时，生命会自我更新。

def_en: A tray, often made of silver, for serving food or drinks
quote_en: The man held a salver with some letters on it and he waited quietly until Mr. Craven took them. (Chapter 27)
quote_zh: 那人拿着一个托盘，上面放着几封信，他静静地等着，直到克雷文先生把它们拿走。
ex_en: A salver may hold the news that changes everything; we must be brave enough to receive it.
ex_zh: 托盘可能盛着改变一切的消息；我们必须足够勇敢去接受它。

def_en: Forcibly put to an end; kept in
quote_en: They were the sounds of running scuffling feet seeming to chase round and round under the trees, they were strange sounds of lowered suppressed voices—exclamations and smothered joyous cries. (Chapter 27)
quote_zh: 那是奔跑的脚步声，似乎在树下绕着圈追逐，还有奇怪的低沉压抑的声音——惊呼声和压抑的欢乐叫喊声。
ex_en: A suppressed joy will find its way out; happiness cannot be hidden forever.
ex_zh: 被压抑的快乐总会找到出口；幸福无法永远隐藏。

def_en: Rushed and reckless; without thought
quote_en: It was the strangest thing he had ever heard, Archibald Craven thought, as it was poured forth in headlong boy fashion. (Chapter 27)
quote_zh: 阿奇博尔德·克雷文想，这是他听过的最奇怪的事情，它以男孩那不顾一切的方式倾泻而出。
ex_en: A headlong rush may stumble, but a heart guided by love finds its feet again.
ex_zh: 鲁莽的冲刺可能会跌倒，但被爱引导的心总能重新站稳。

def_en: A bird's feathers
quote_en: When she had reached the place where the door hid itself under the ivy, she was startled by a curious loud sound. It was the caw—caw of a crow and it came from the top of the wall, and when she looked up, there sat a big glossy-plumaged blue-black bird, looking down at her very wisely indeed. (Chapter 15)
quote_zh: 当她走到门隐藏在常春藤下的地方时，一声奇怪而响亮的声音吓了她一跳。那是乌鸦的"嘎——嘎"声，从墙头传来，她抬头一看，一只羽毛光滑的蓝黑色大鸟坐在那里，确实非常智慧地俯视着她。
ex_en: A bird's plumage may be bright, but its song is what truly delights the heart.
ex_zh: 鸟儿的羽毛或许鲜艳，但它的歌声才能真正愉悦心灵。

def_en: To make untidy; to rumple
quote_en: He got up himself, laughing and glowing, and tousled; his eyes like a bit of the sky. (Chapter 15)
quote_zh: 他自己站了起来，笑着，满脸红光，头发乱蓬蓬的；他的眼睛像一片天空。
ex_en: A tousled head means a day well spent; order can wait, but joy will not.
ex_zh: 蓬乱的头发意味着一天过得充实；整齐可以等待，但快乐不会。

def_en: A feeling of intense pleasure or joy
quote_en: They put their eager young noses close to the earth and sniffed its warmed springtime breathing; they dug and pulled and laughed low with rapture until Mistress Mary's hair was as tumbled as Dickon's and her cheeks were almost as poppy red as his. (Chapter 15)
quote_zh: 他们把急切的小鼻子贴近泥土，嗅着它温暖的春天气息；他们挖着、拔着，压抑着狂喜低声笑着，直到玛丽小姐的头发变得和迪肯的一样蓬乱，脸颊也几乎和他一样红如罂粟。
ex_en: Rapture needs no reason; it is the heart's answer to beauty found.
ex_zh: 狂喜不需要理由；它是心灵对发现美的回应。

def_en: An idea, belief, or opinion
quote_en: "Us mustn't seem as if us was watchin' him too close," said Dickon. "He'd be out with us for good if he got th' notion us was interferin' now." (Chapter 15)
quote_zh: "咱们不能显得像是在太近地盯着它看，"迪肯说。"要是它觉得咱们现在在打扰它，就再也不会跟咱们待在一起了。"
ex_en: A single notion, like a seed, can grow into a forest of change if we let it.
ex_zh: 一个念头，如同种子，若我们任其生长，便可长成一片改变的森林。

def_en: Expressing sorrow; mournful
quote_en: She ran back to the house as quickly as her feet would carry her. She wanted to tell Colin about Dickon's fox cub and the rook and about what the springtime had been doing... So it was not very pleasant when she opened the door of her room, to see Martha standing waiting for her with a doleful face. (Chapter 16)
quote_zh: 她以最快的速度跑回房子。她想告诉科林关于迪肯的小狐狸崽和白嘴鸦的事，还有春天在做什么……所以，当她打开房门，看到玛莎站在那里，一脸忧愁地等着她时，感觉可不怎么好。
ex_en: A doleful face may hide a heart that simply needs a story or a friend to lift it.
ex_zh: 忧愁的面孔背后，或许藏着一颗只需一个故事或一位朋友就能振作的心。

def_en: To do something in a haughty way; to lower oneself
quote_en: Colin frowned and condescended to look at her. (Chapter 16)
quote_zh: 科林皱了皱眉，屈尊地看了她一眼。
ex_en: Those who condescend build walls; those who kneel to help build bridges that last.
ex_zh: 屈尊俯就的人建造高墙；跪下帮助的人架起持久的桥梁。

def_en: To stare in an angry or fierce way
quote_en: They were a nice agreeable pair as they glared at each other. (Chapter 16)
quote_zh: 他们怒视着对方，真是般配的一对。
ex_en: A glare may win a battle, but a kind word wins a friend for all the days to come.
ex_zh: 怒视或许能赢得一场争斗，但善意的话语能赢得一生的朋友。

def_en: To deny or oppose what someone has said
quote_en: "You're not!" contradicted Mary unsympathetically. (Chapter 16)
quote_zh: "你不是！"玛丽不客气地反驳道。
ex_en: To contradict may clear the air, but to listen opens the door to true understanding.
ex_zh: 反驳或许能澄清事实，但倾听才能打开真正理解之门。

def_en: To trouble or afflict; to be ill
quote_en: "Hysterics and temper are half what ails him." (Chapter 16)
quote_zh: "歇斯底里和坏脾气占了他毛病的一半。"
ex_en: What ails the body can be healed; what ails the spirit needs the sunlight of kindness.
ex_zh: 身体的病痛可以治愈；精神的困扰需要善意的阳光。

def_en: Not yielding in strength or determination; harsh
quote_en: She would never tell him and he could stay in his room and never get any fresh air and die if he liked! It would serve him right! She felt so sour and unrelenting that for a few minutes she almost forgot about Dickon and the green veil creeping over the world and the soft wind blowing down from the moor. (Chapter 16)
quote_zh: 她永远不会告诉他，他爱待在他房间里就待着，永远不呼吸新鲜空气，爱死就死吧！活该！她感到如此刻薄和无情，以至于有几分钟几乎忘记了迪肯、蔓延到世界上的绿色面纱，以及从荒野吹来的温柔的风。
ex_en: An unrelenting heart may win its way, but a flexible one wins the love that makes the journey worthwhile.
ex_zh: 无情的心或许能赢得胜利，但柔软的心能赢得让这段旅程值得的爱。

def_en: To twist or squirm in pain or discomfort
quote_en: "I felt the lump—I felt it," choked out Colin. "I knew I should. I shall have a hunch on my back and then I shall die," and he began to writhe again and turned on his face and sobbed and wailed but he didn't scream. (Chapter 17)
quote_zh: "我摸到那个肿块了——我摸到了，"科林哽咽着说。"我就知道会有。我的背上会长出驼背，然后我就会死。"他又开始扭动起来，翻过身去，抽泣哀号着，但没有尖叫。
ex_en: Even when fear makes us writhe, a steady hand and a calm voice can call us back to hope.
ex_zh: 即使在恐惧让我们痛苦挣扎时，一只稳定的手和冷静的声音也能将我们唤回希望。

def_en: To make partial changes; to alter
quote_en: When he was very much interested he often spoke quite broad Yorkshire though at other times he tried to modify his dialect so that Mary could better understand. (Chapter 18)
quote_zh: 当他非常感兴趣时，他常常说很浓重的约克郡方言，尽管其他时候他试图改变他的口音，以便玛丽能更好地理解。
ex_en: We modify our ways not to hide who we are, but to open our hearts to those who are different.
ex_zh: 我们改变自己的方式不是为了隐藏自己，而是向那些与我们不同的人敞开心扉。

def_en: To rub or push gently with the nose or face
quote_en: He walked over to Colin's sofa and put the new-born lamb quietly on his lap, and immediately the little creature turned to the warm velvet dressing-gown and began to nuzzle and nuzzle into its folds and butt its tight-curled head with soft impatience against his side. (Chapter 19)
quote_zh: 他走到科林的沙发前，轻轻地把新生的小羊放在他的膝上，小家伙立刻转向温暖的天鹅绒晨衣，开始用鼻子在衣褶里蹭啊蹭，用柔软的、不耐烦的动作把卷曲的小脑袋抵在他身上。
ex_en: To nuzzle close to another is to say, without words, "You are safe with me."
ex_zh: 用鼻尖蹭着靠近，是在无言地说："在我身边你是安全的。"

def_en: Lean, tough, and strong
quote_en: He was a tiny little shaggy moor pony with thick locks hanging over his eyes and with a pretty face and a nuzzling velvet nose. He was rather thin with living on moor grass but he was as tough and wiry as if the muscle in his little legs had been made of steel springs. (Chapter 18)
quote_zh: 那是一匹小小的、毛茸茸的荒野小马，厚厚的鬃毛垂在眼前，脸很漂亮，鼻子是毛茸茸的天鹅绒。靠吃荒野的草为生，它相当瘦，但却坚韧结实，仿佛它小腿上的肌肉是用钢弹簧做的。
ex_en: A wiry spirit may seem small, but it holds the strength to climb the highest hills.
ex_zh: 坚韧的精神或许看起来渺小，却蕴含着攀登最高山峰的力量。

def_en: To make the gentle, high-pitched sound of a horse
